Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist

Location: Remote




About Gini Talent

Gini Talent is a global talent solutions company recognized among the world's leading IT recruitment and staffing firms. We specialize in recruitment, staffing, Employer of Record (EOR), and Professional Employer Organization (PEO) services, helping organizations scale and succeed across international markets.




With more than 3,000 employees operating across 11 countries and serving clients in 14+ markets, we connect exceptional talent with innovative companies worldwide.




About the Role

We are looking for a Data Scientist to join a highly collaborative team focused on developing innovative data science solutions and transforming complex datasets into actionable business insights.




In this role, you will contribute to the full product lifecycle, from ideation and prototyping to model development, deployment, and continuous improvement. You will work closely with data scientists, analysts, and domain experts to build scalable and impactful machine learning solutions.




This position is ideal for candidates who enjoy solving challenging business problems, collaborating across disciplines, and continuously exploring new technologies and methodologies.




Responsibilities

Contribute to brainstorming, ideation, and prototyping activities for new data science products.
Develop and enhance machine learning and statistical models to address business challenges.
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and domain experts to build scalable analytical solutions.
Improve and maintain existing codebases and data science products.
Apply statistical and machine learning techniques to real-world datasets.
Support knowledge-sharing initiatives and contribute to team competency development.
Promote data science best practices and contribute to continuous improvement efforts.
Engage with end users and stakeholders to gather feedback and translate requirements into product enhancements.
Participate actively in Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, reviews, and code discussions.




Requirements

Statistics & Analytics

Strong understanding of statistical theory, methods, and algorithms.
Experience applying statistical techniques to real-world datasets.
Ability to work with challenges related to sampling limitations, bias, and data quality.




Data Science & Machine Learning

Experience building and optimizing predictive models.
Hands-on expertise in regression and classification problems.
Strong understanding of machine learning model evaluation techniques.
Experience addressing class imbalance, weighting strategies, and other data quality challenges.
Practical experience applying machine learning models in production or business environments.




Software Development

Experience with software development best practices, testing frameworks, and CI/CD processes.
Familiarity with GitHub-based development workflows, including code reviews and version control.
Understanding of modern development practices such as atomic commits and conventional commits.
Interest in AI-assisted development tools such as GitHub Copilot.




Technical Skills

Required

Python (NumPy, SciPy, Scikit-learn)
GitHub
Azure Machine Learning and/or equivalent open-source machine learning platforms




Nice to Have

Time Series Analysis
Geospatial Analytics
Geoscience or Subsurface Data Knowledge
Streamlit or similar data application frameworks




Soft Skills

Strong collaboration and teamwork mindset.
Curious, analytical, and creative problem-solving approach.
Excellent communication skills and ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Passion for learning, mentoring, and knowledge sharing.
Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments is a plus.




If you are passionate about transforming data into meaningful business impact and enjoy working in a collaborative, innovation-driven environment, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist / BI Developer (Power BI & Python)

📍 Remote | Long-term Project | International Environment




Are you passionate about turning complex data into meaningful business insights?

We're looking for a Data Scientist / BI Developer with strong Power BI and Python skills to join a data-driven team delivering modern analytics and reporting solutions. You'll work across the full lifecycle of business intelligence initiatives - from understanding stakeholder needs to building dashboards, automating data flows, and driving continuous improvements.




What You'll Do

Design and develop engaging Power BI dashboards and reports.
Transform business requirements into actionable data solutions.
Build and enhance data models, KPIs, and analytical datasets.
Develop and maintain Python-based data processing pipelines.
Integrate data from multiple sources and ensure data quality.
Create scalable reporting solutions supporting strategic decision-making.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, analysts, and technical teams.
Troubleshoot, optimize, and continuously improve existing solutions.
Produce clear technical and functional documentation.
Occasionally travel to Brussel's office.




What We're Looking For

Hands-on experience with Power BI development.
Strong knowledge of DAX and Power Query.
Good Python programming skills.
Experience with ETL processes, data pipelines, or data engineering activities.
Understanding of data modelling and business intelligence principles.
Ability to gather and analyse business requirements.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication skills in English.




Nice to Have

Experience with AI-powered analytics or data classification.
Background in enterprise-scale reporting environments.
Exposure to cloud data platforms.
Experience working with large or complex datasets.
Knowledge of data governance and quality management practices.




Why Join?

Work on impactful, data-centric projects.
Collaborate with international teams and stakeholders.
Exposure to modern BI, analytics, and data engineering technologies.
Flexible remote working model.
Opportunity to shape reporting solutions used for strategic decision-making.
Long-term cooperation and professional growth opportunities.




If you enjoy combining data engineering, analytics, and business-facing work while building solutions that create real value, we'd love to hear from you. 🚀

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Key Responsibilities:

Collect, clean, and preprocess structured and unstructured data from multiple sources.
Analyze large datasets to identify patterns, trends, and insights.
Support the development and validation of predictive models and machine learning algorithms.
Visualize data using tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Matplotlib/Seaborn.
Assist in designing and implementing data pipelines and workflows.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into data-driven solutions.
Document code, processes, and findings clearly and accurately.
Stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies in data science and analytics.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis and modeling.
Familiarity with data manipulation libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy) and basic machine learning frameworks (e.g., scikit-learn, TensorFlow).
Basic understanding of statistics, probability, and data modeling concepts.
Experience with SQL and working with databases.
Good communication skills and the ability to explain complex findings to non-technical stakeholders.
Salary proposed: €50,000 - €60,000

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



What you will do

As an expert in the field of Machine Learning Engineering you will be responsible for the growth of our Data team, both professionally as well as in size. You will be in charge of shaping and executing the future of your expertise within Sia. You will work on client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our Dutch team of Data Scientists, our French Data Engineering team, and other business units of Sia Global.



Your responsibilities include:



Leading an internal team towards the development of AI products;
Working in a multidisciplinary team on client projects as an expert on your subject;
Making architectural and design decisions for cloud environments;
Building production level ML/AI solutions with solid software engineering;
Guiding Data Scientists to scale up their models into production (parallelizing, optimizing, tuning, testing, and wrapping);
Giving trainings, leading internal meetings and organizing external events;
Participating in team meetings and workshops with clients;
Writing blog articles, presenting at conferences and sending out press releases on the subject that you lead.

Who you will work with

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing (10+). You will join a team that will challenge you and invest in your professional development. We have a flat structure and rely on the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we can grow together.



Who you are



You are an established technical professional that wants to make the next step in their career, taking on more leadership responsibilities.
You have first-rate project management and time management skills.
You are a true problem solver, you turn complex issues into simple and impactful business solutions.
You are enthusiastic for applying Data Science/Engineering solutions to business problems.
You have excellent communication skills and can present complicated concepts in a synthetic way.
You have the ability to adapt rapidly and work on a range of different business areas within a challenging environment.
You have the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


MSc level education in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems or related field of study.
2-4 years of experience in building production-quality ML/Deep Learning pipelines and models.
Good understanding of Machine Learning fundamentals and strong knowledge in a specific domain (e.g., Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP).
Expert coding skills in Python and at least one of these languages: Scala, C++, Java.
Hands-on experience with database management languages (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL).
Hands-on experience in cloud-based infrastructures (AWS/GCP/Azure).
Hands-on experience with Unix-based command line and DevOps tools (Git, Docker, Kubernetes).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, Databricks).
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Experience with coaching/mentoring other engineers.
Prior experience in Management Consulting is a strong plus.
Willingness to travel and work at local and international clients.
Fluency in spoken and written English, French and Dutch is required.


Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI
Description:
Application deadline: August 31, 2026

About Us

VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.

We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI.

Your role

As An ML Engineer, You Will


Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.
Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.
Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.
Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.
Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment.


Your profile


PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.
Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.
Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.
We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers.


Our offer


A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.
An environment that promotes personal development.
The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.
Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.
The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society.


About VIB.AI

VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en.

How to apply

Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents:


Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate
to the current position.
Your CV.
Contact information of three references.


Application deadline: August 31st 2026.

For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
I'm currently working with an innovative company at the forefront of the AgriTech sector, leveraging AI and machine learning to transform the future of farming and sustainable food production. They are looking to hire a Machine Learning Engineer to help build intelligent solutions that improve crop yields, optimise resource usage, and drive greater efficiency across agricultural operations.




This is an opportunity to apply cutting-edge machine learning techniques to real-world challenges, helping shape a more sustainable and data-driven farming industry.

The Role




As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll play a key role in developing and deploying production-grade ML solutions that support precision agriculture and smart farming initiatives. You'll work alongside software engineers, data scientists, and domain experts to deliver scalable systems capable of processing large volumes of sensor, satellite, and operational data.




Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy machine learning models into production environments.
Develop data pipelines capable of processing large-scale agricultural and environmental datasets.
Build predictive models to improve crop health, yield forecasting, irrigation optimisation, and resource management.
Work with structured and unstructured data from IoT devices, sensors, drones, and satellite imagery.
Collaborate with engineering and product teams to translate business challenges into AI-driven solutions.
Implement MLOps best practices, including model monitoring, retraining, and automated deployment.
Explore emerging technologies including Generative AI and advanced analytics to enhance farming operations.




What They're Looking For

Essential Experience

3+ years of commercial experience building machine learning solutions.
Strong Python development skills.
Experience with PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, or similar ML frameworks.
Strong understanding of feature engineering, model evaluation, and optimisation techniques.
Experience working with large datasets and SQL databases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work within cross-functional teams.

Desirable

Experience working with geospatial data, satellite imagery, computer vision, or IoT datasets.
Exposure to time-series forecasting and predictive analytics.
Experience with Generative AI, LLMs, or MLOps platforms such as MLflow or Kubeflow.
Knowledge of distributed computing frameworks such as Spark or Ray.




Why Join?

Agriculture is undergoing a technological revolution, and my client is leading that change. Their mission is to empower farmers with intelligent tools that increase productivity, reduce waste, and support more sustainable food production.

This is an opportunity to work on meaningful problems where your models will have a tangible impact—not only on business performance but on the future of global agriculture.




If you're a Machine Learning Engineer looking to apply AI to one of the world's most essential industries, I'd be keen to speak with you.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
About the Company

Established in 2004, ALLSTARSIT was founded with a clear vision: to enhance the landscape of global IT employment by bridging the gap between companies and skilled professionals. The core belief was that assembling a team shouldn't be hindered by geographical constraints. Fast forward to the present day, ALLSTARSIT stands as an international outstaffing service provider committed to change the way businesses recruit, compensate, and oversee top talent worldwide.




With operational hubs scattered across Europe, Asia, and LATAM, and its headquarters situated in San Francisco, US, the company boasts a workforce of over 1,000 adept professionals. Spanning across more than 20 countries, ALLSTARSIT offers a diverse range of skilled employees across various verticals, including AI, cybersecurity, healthcare, fintech, telecom, media, and so on.

About the Project

You will be part of a research team, directly impacting the fundamentals of our data research, supporting the creation and improvement of our core-data algorithms.
As part of your role, you will be the driving force to scale our AI capabilities.
You will own the infrastructure and processes that support the full lifecycle of AI models - from research through production and performance monitoring.

Required skills:

B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Computer Science or Software Engineering or related field
Experienced with ML/DL workflows and their best practices
Experienced with CI/CD workflows and their best practices
Worked with public cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP)
Experienced with Python and Java
Experience with various data stores like Postgres, MongoDB, Redis
Experience with DS tools such as MLFlow, Langfuse, SageMaker, etc.
Experience with Spark
Experience with PyTorch/TensorFlow

Scope of work:

Lead and drive the deployment, lifecycle management, and monitoring of ML/DL models in all stages leading to production.
Design and implement systems for Dataset and Label Management, including versioning and integrating customer feedback into labeling workflows.
Establish and maintain a robust Model Repository/Registry that supports versioning, local inference, and model lineage.
Lead the implementation of advanced Experiment Tracking and Monitoring solutions for both Data Science and Generative AI, focusing on evaluation, data drift detection, and model reproducibility.
Own model serving and inference systems—including autoscaling, A/B testing, canary rollouts, and latency/cost optimization for production models.
Enable specialized infrastructure for Generative AI capabilities, including tagging tools, prompt management, and LLM testing services.
Drive operational excellence by improving tool deployment usability and implementing granular cost visibility across projects and environments.
Developing reusable components such as standardized data loaders, CI/CD pipelines, and automated workflows for tasks like model retraining.
Collaborate directly with Data Scientists and the rest of the Data Platform Engineering team to productionize ML/DL models developed for cloud environments.

Title: Senior Data Scientist (Machine Learning & AI)
Description:
At Ayesa Digital we grow with you!




Every professional in our company is vital to us. Thanks to their talent, we continue to expand; today, we are a global team of over 11,000 people working toward a common goal.




Ayesa Digital is currently participating in high-impact European Union projects designed to address major European challenges and drive science and innovation. These are strategic technological projects based on collaborative initiatives that stand out for their international focus and a strong commitment to socially-oriented results.




International project focused on the evolution and operation of a large-scale portfolio of digital solutions supporting complex business processes, including procurement, programme management, and data analytics.

The scope covers the full digital solution lifecycle, from design and development to deployment, operations, and continuous improvement, leveraging cloud environments and Agile/DevSecOps practices.

This is a highly technical and innovative environment, focused on scalable, secure, and data-driven architectures, with strong emphasis on interoperability, service quality, and compliance with European standards in security and data protection




If you are an enthusiastic professional looking for a new career challenge, this is your place. We are looking to incorporate an Senior Data Scientist (Machine Learning & AI). Leap — we are waiting for you!




What You Will Do (Responsibilities):

Analyse business and customer needs and translate them into data-driven solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data is clean, accurate, and accessible.
Identify patterns and trends in large datasets to uncover actionable insights.
Design and develop data models to improve decision-making processes.
Build, train, and deploy machine learning models in production environments.
Monitor model performance and continuously optimise and improve results.
Implement and deploy data tools for advanced data analysis.
Work closely with developers to integrate machine learning solutions into products and services.




What We Are Looking For (Requirements):

Minimum of 8 years of professional experience after completing higher education.
At least 4 years of studies attested by a relevant diploma.
Strong experience with programming languages such as Python or R.
Hands-on expertise with data visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Experience working with Big Data frameworks such as Spark, Hadoop, Databricks, or Splunk.
Knowledge of AI/ML frameworks and tools (e.g., TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, CNTK, RAG techniques).
Proven ability to deploy machine learning models in production environments.
Strong analytical mindset and ability to work collaboratively with technical and business teams.




What We Offer:

Prestigious projects within European institutions.
International, innovative, and multicultural environments.
Continuous support from a team of experts in EU projects.
Rate per day: 571€ - 587€ (Negotiable depending on the candidate’s experience.)




If you are ambitious, enthusiastic, and seeking a new professional challenge in international projects with real-world impact, this is the place for you!




In accordance with Organic Law 3/2007 of March 22, the company is committed to promoting the defense and effective application of the principle of equality between men and women, preventing any type of labor discrimination based on sex, and guaranteeing equal entry opportunities. Furthermore, we promote diversity and reject any discrimination based on race, gender, functional diversity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other personal or social condition, striving to build an inclusive and enriching environment.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist.
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist

Description du poste

Au sein de l’équipe AI & Analytics Solutions, nous souhaitons placer la prise de décision intelligente (assistée par l’humain ou automatisée) au cœur de nos processus et parcours métier, en développant une culture de la donnée et de l’expérimentation qui pourrait devenir un véritable avantage concurrentiel dans un monde en constante évolution.

Vous êtes passionné(e) par l’exploration des données et leur transformation en solutions à fort impact ? Vous aimez créer de la valeur métier à partir des données ?

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist, vous serez amené(e) à :


Collaborer étroitement avec les parties prenantes métier afin de comprendre leurs enjeux et les traduire en problématiques analytiques.
Appliquer des techniques de machine learning, des méthodes statistiques et des approches d’optimisation afin de développer des solutions innovantes générant un impact métier concret.
Concevoir, développer et évaluer des modèles prédictifs et prescriptifs, en garantissant leur fiabilité et leur robustesse.
Ajuster, adapter et évaluer des modèles de langage de grande taille (LLM) et des modèles Vision-Langage (VLM) à poids ouverts pour des cas d’usage en entreprise, en utilisant notamment des techniques telles que le fine-tuning supervisé, le parameter-efficient tuning (LoRA/QLoRA), l’instruction tuning et l’évaluation de modèles.
Piloter l’ensemble du cycle de vie des produits de machine learning : de l’idéation et du prototypage jusqu’au déploiement et au suivi continu en production.
Collaborer avec les autres Data Scientists afin de partager les connaissances, les bonnes pratiques et d’améliorer continuellement nos méthodologies.
Garantir la qualité et la performance des solutions grâce à des tests rigoureux et un monitoring continu après le déploiement.
Contribuer à notre communauté Data Science en développant des actifs réutilisables, en partageant votre expertise et en participant aux initiatives d’innovation.


Profil

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist, vous disposez de :


Minimum 2 ans d’expérience en tant que Data Scientist, ML Engineer ou dans une fonction analytique similaire.
Un diplôme de Master dans un domaine pertinent (par exemple : mathématiques appliquées, statistiques, data science, physique ou informatique).
Une solide maîtrise pratique du machine learning, des statistiques et des techniques d’optimisation, avec une expérience concrète de leur application à des problématiques réelles. Une expérience dans le développement, l’évaluation et le déploiement de solutions Data Science de bout en bout constitue un atout essentiel.
Une expérience pratique des modèles de fondation (foundation models), incluant l’ajustement, l’adaptation, l’évaluation et le déploiement de modèles LLM et/ou VLM à poids ouverts.
Une bonne connaissance des frameworks et techniques modernes de GenAI tels que Hugging Face, Transformers, PEFT/LoRA, le model serving, la génération augmentée par récupération (RAG) et les méthodologies d’évaluation des LLM.
Une excellente maîtrise de Python ainsi que des bibliothèques courantes de Data Science et de machine learning.
Une connaissance des environnements cloud ainsi que des pratiques MLOps et LLMOps.
D’excellentes compétences en communication, avec la capacité d’expliquer des concepts complexes à des publics techniques comme non techniques.
Une parfaite maîtrise de l’anglais, ainsi que du français ou du néerlandais.


Et vous êtes également :


Passionné(e) par la résolution de problématiques métier grâce à la donnée et à l’analytique.
Orienté(e) résultats, capable de concilier excellence technique et besoins métier.
Curieux(se) et désireux(se) d’apprendre en continu et d’expérimenter de nouvelles approches.
Collaboratif(ve) et à l’aise dans les environnements de travail en équipe.
Proactif(ve), analytique et orienté(e) solutions.


AXA souhaite refléter la diversité de la société dans laquelle nous vivons et mène donc depuis des années une politique active en faveur de la diversité, de la non-discrimination et de l'égalité des chances. Dans ce cadre, nous garantissons un suivi égal et un accès équitable au recrutement, indépendamment de l'âge, de l'origine, du contexte culturel, du handicap, du genre, de l'orientation sexuelle ou d'autres caractéristiques.

AXA Belgium

Nous sommes plus de 3000 employés, travaillant dans nos bureaux modernes situés à Bruxelles, Anvers, Eupen et Liège. Nous servons 3 millions de clients en partenariat avec 3200 courtiers et nous sommes fiers d’être le premier assureur auto du pays. Notre ambition est de ne pas seulement être un payeur, mais plutôt un véritable partenaire pour nos clients.

Nos quatre valeurs principales nous poussent chaque jour à donner le meilleur de nous-mêmes pour nos clients, tout en collaborant efficacement avec nos collègues: Customer First, Integrity, Courage et One AXA

Chez AXA, nous rejetons toute forme de discrimination déloyale ou illégale. Plus d’informations sur notre politique de Diversité et d’Inclusion.

Ce que nous vous offrons

Une rémunération et des avantages en lien avec votre travail

Vous bénéficierez d’un salaire compétitif, complété éventuellement d’un bonus discrétionnaire lié au résultat personnel et d’un bonus collectif, avec des chèques-repas, des écochèques, du remboursement total des transports en commun et un plan cafétaria.

Tous les avantages d’un assureur comme employeur

Vous bénéficierez d’une assurance pension et d’une assurance hospitalisation et soins ambulatoires (également à un tarif préférentiel pour les membres de votre ménage) ainsi que d’une réduction allant jusqu'à 30 % sur nos autres produits d'assurance.

Un emploi qui s'adapte à votre vie et à vos rêves

Un horaire flexible de 35 heures par semaine, 36 jours de congé par an pour vous ressourcer, ainsi que la possibilité de travailler partiellement depuis la maison.

Vous bénéficierez de cours de sport au travail, de programmes de bien-être pour les employés et de formations, afin de vous maintenir en pleine forme, tant physiquement que mentalement.

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist, vous serez amené(e) à :

  • Collaborer étroitement avec les parties prenantes métier afin de comprendre leurs enjeux et les traduire en problématiques analytiques.
  • Appliquer des techniques de machine learning, des méthodes statistiques et des approches d’optimisation afin de développer des solutions innovantes générant un impact métier concret.
  • Concevoir, développer et évaluer des modèles prédictifs et prescriptifs, en garantissant leur fiabilité et leur robustesse.
  • Ajuster, adapter et évaluer des modèles de langage de grande taille (LLM) et des modèles Vision-Langage (VLM) à poids ouverts pour des cas d’usage en entreprise, en utilisant notamment des techniques telles que le fine-tuning supervisé, le parameter-efficient tuning (LoRA/QLoRA), l’instruction tuning et l’évaluation de modèles.
  • Piloter l’ensemble du cycle de vie des produits de machine learning : de l’idéation et du prototypage jusqu’au déploiement et au suivi continu en production.
  • Collaborer avec les autres Data Scientists afin de partager les connaissances, les bonnes pratiques et d’améliorer continuellement nos méthodologies.
  • Garantir la qualité et la performance des solutions grâce à des tests rigoureux et un monitoring continu après le déploiement.
  • Contribuer à notre communauté Data Science en développant des actifs réutilisables, en partageant votre expertise et en participant aux initiatives d’innovation.

Profil

En tant que Machine Learning Engineer - Data Scientist, vous disposez de :

  • Minimum 2 ans d’expérience en tant que Data Scientist, ML Engineer ou dans une fonction analytique similaire.
  • Un diplôme de Master dans un domaine pertinent (par exemple : mathématiques appliquées, statistiques, data science, physique ou informatique).
  • Une solide maîtrise pratique du machine learning, des statistiques et des techniques d’optimisation, avec une expérience concrète de leur application à des problématiques réelles. Une expérience dans le développement, l’évaluation et le déploiement de solutions Data Science de bout en bout constitue un atout essentiel.
  • Une expérience pratique des modèles de fondation (foundation models), incluant l’ajustement, l’adaptation, l’évaluation et le déploiement de modèles LLM et/ou VLM à poids ouverts.
  • Une bonne connaissance des frameworks et techniques modernes de GenAI tels que Hugging Face, Transformers, PEFT/LoRA, le model serving, la génération augmentée par récupération (RAG) et les méthodologies d’évaluation des LLM.
  • Une excellente maîtrise de Python ainsi que des bibliothèques courantes de Data Science et de machine learning.
  • Une connaissance des environnements cloud ainsi que des pratiques MLOps et LLMOps.
  • D’excellentes compétences en communication, avec la capacité d’expliquer des concepts complexes à des publics techniques comme non techniques.
  • Une parfaite maîtrise de l’anglais, ainsi que du français ou du néerlandais.

Et vous êtes également :

  • Passionné(e) par la résolution de problématiques métier grâce à la donnée et à l’analytique.
  • Orienté(e) résultats, capable de concilier excellence technique et besoins métier.
  • Curieux(se) et désireux(se) d’apprendre en continu et d’expérimenter de nouvelles approches.
  • Collaboratif(ve) et à l’aise dans les environnements de travail en équipe.
  • Proactif(ve), analytique et orienté(e) solutions.

AXA souhaite refléter la diversité de la société dans laquelle nous vivons et mène donc depuis des années une politique active en faveur de la diversité, de la non-discrimination et de l'égalité des chances. Dans ce cadre, nous garantissons un suivi égal et un accès équitable au recrutement, indépendamment de l'âge, de l'origine, du contexte culturel, du handicap, du genre, de l'orientation sexuelle ou d'autres caractéristiques.

AXA Belgium

Nous sommes plus de 3000 employés, travaillant dans nos bureaux modernes situés à Bruxelles, Anvers, Eupen et Liège. Nous servons 3 millions de clients en partenariat avec 3200 courtiers et nous sommes fiers d’être le premier assureur auto du pays. Notre ambition est de ne pas seulement être un payeur, mais plutôt un véritable partenaire pour nos clients.

Nos quatre valeurs principales nous poussent chaque jour à donner le meilleur de nous-mêmes pour nos clients, tout en collaborant efficacement avec nos collègues: Customer First, Integrity, Courage et One AXA

Chez AXA, nous rejetons toute forme de discrimination déloyale ou illégale. Plus d’informations sur notre politique de Diversité et d’Inclusion.

Ce que nous vous offrons

Une rémunération et des avantages en lien avec votre travail

Vous bénéficierez d’un salaire compétitif, complété éventuellement d’un bonus discrétionnaire lié au résultat personnel et d’un bonus collectif, avec des chèques-repas, des écochèques, du remboursement total des transports en commun et un plan cafétaria.

Tous les avantages d’un assureur comme employeur

Vous bénéficierez d’une assurance pension et d’une assurance hospitalisation et soins ambulatoires (également à un tarif préférentiel pour les membres de votre ménage) ainsi que d’une réduction allant jusqu'à 30 % sur nos autres produits d'assurance.

Un emploi qui s'adapte à votre vie et à vos rêves

Un horaire flexible de 35 heures par semaine, 36 jours de congé par an pour vous ressourcer, ainsi que la possibilité de travailler partiellement depuis la maison.

Vous bénéficierez de cours de sport au travail, de programmes de bien-être pour les employés et de formations, afin de vous maintenir en pleine forme, tant physiquement que mentalement.

Title: Data Science Internship
HTML:
Job Location

Brussels

Job Description

Do you have passion for technology and desire to learn and grow continuously? Do you want to belong to one of the most advanced Data Science & Analytics group in the industry, developing sophisticated mathematical models and algorithms and applying Operations Research techniques?

Then join P&G as a Data Science intern for Supply Chain! Through your internship, you will build your Data Science and Analytics skills, develop your ability to influence and think strategically and learn how to work across domains and functions to make P&G’s supply chain more performant, more resilient and more sustainable.

Find out more about IT at P&G at www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ial3aeA9SE.

AS A DATA SCIENCE FOR SUPPLY CHAIN INTERN, YOU WILL:

  • Influence decisions from daily through to strategic ones, made across functions, at all levels of leadership, to have a meaningful impact on the performance, resiliency and environmental footprint of one of the largest CPG companies in the world;
  • Build the next generation of supply chain analytics, using:
    • Mathematical modeling techniques: decision analysis, optimization, machine learning, simulation etc.;
    • Programming languages (at least one of these): Python, SQL, R, Hive/Impala, MATLAB, etc.;
    • Data Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, etc.
    • Work with technology partners to translate your innovations into robust, scaled, analytical solutions;
    • Communicate insights from complex data or algorithms into simple conclusions that will empower others to act based on the insights you derive;
    • Belong to an award-winning Analytics / Operations Research community that is global in scope and operates across P&G’s supply chains.

    Job Qualifications

    YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF YOU:

    • Are currently pursuing a Master's degree in a quantitative field (Engineering, Statistics, Operation Research, Decisions Sciences, Computer Science, Applied Math, Economics, Data Science, etc.);
    • Have strong analytical skills and proficiency in at least one programming language (R, Python, SQL, etc.) or family of techniques (optimization, simulation, machine learning, predictive modeling, etc.);
    • Are adaptable and able to handle multiple priorities effectively, both independently and as part of a collaborative team;
    • Are proficient in English, both written and spoken;

    We are offering internships of at least 3 months throughout the year, as of May 2026.

    If studying at a Belgian university, please make sure you consult with the internship department at your school to agree on the paid internship we offer.

    Your Advantages

    • A temporary contract for the duration of your internship, with an attractive compensation package;
    • Monthly allowance: 2366 EUR brut/month, prorated 13th month based on the months worked during your internship period;
    • A flexible benefits plan to customize to your personal needs and kilometer allowance/public transport reduction;
    • Access to Benefits at work platform: A large benefits platform with employee discounts on renowned shops and online commercial platforms as well as on leisure activities and well-known amusement parks;
    • Working in an international and inclusive environment, with diverse and supporting colleagues;
    • Continuous self-development via coaching, trainings and a general Growth Mindset working environment;
    • A vibrant and modern working environment, supporting employees’ wellbeing:
    • Subsidized company restaurant;
    • On-site vibrant Café for breaks and less formal meetings;
    • On-site fitness room and possibility to join regular group sport activities;
    • Flexibility in terms of working hours;
    • Employee Assistance Program: free professional counseling services, work-life resources, and mental health support.
    • Supporting employee's commute: plenty of parking facilities for both cars and bikes, shower facilities, shuttle between Heizel metro and the office, carpooling community.

    ARE YOU EXCITED ABOUT THE ROLE? DEEP DIVE INTO P&G AND CHECK THE BELOW INSIGHTFUL LINKS:

    • Our hiring process consists of three main steps: 1) application, 2) online assessments and 3) interviews. Click here to get all the tips to be fully prepared.
    • P&G is driven to make life better, not just within the company, but across the globe. Click here to check out how we make a meaningful impact on the world.
    • We are the world’s largest consumer goods company. Click here to discover about our iconic brands.

    At P&G #weseeequal

    We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

    We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process. Please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment, we thank you in advance for your patience.

    R000145698

    €2.414,00 / month

Title: Data scientist _FR
Description:
Comment pouvez-vous faire la différence ?

En tant que Data scientist – dans le service Corporate strategy, vous développez et maintenez des modèles stratégiques qui fournissent des insights approfondis et qui contribuent directement aux choix politiques et aux décisions d’investissement.

Vous travaillez à l’intersection des données, du business et de la stratégie et vous veillez à ce que des hypothèses et calculs complexes soient implémentés de manière transparente, robuste et reproductible dans des solutions de données évolutives.

Quel est votre rôle à la SNCB ?

En tant que Data scientist, vous aurez les responsabilités suivantes :


Vous développez, maintenez et industrialisez des modèles stratégiques sur la base d’hypothèses métier, en collaboration avec les parties prenantes concernées.
Vous travaillez en étroite collaboration avec des collègues de différentes directions de la SNCB dans un contexte transversal à l’échelle de toute l’organisation.
Vous traduisez des modèles Excel existants, aujourd’hui gérés localement, en solutions PySpark robustes et évolutives.
Vous automatisez le traitement des données et l’exécution des modèles via des pipelines dans Databricks.
Vous travaillez avec Python et PySpark dans un environnement Azure Databricks pour traiter les données, implémenter des modèles et réaliser des analyses.
Vous veillez à la transparence, à la reproductibilité et au respect des bonnes pratiques lors de l’implémentation des modèles.
Vous explorez les opportunités d’intégrer des sources de données et des techniques analytiques supplémentaires (telles que les données spatiales, l’IA ou des visualisations avancées) lorsqu’elles apportent une réelle valeur ajoutée aux insights stratégiques.
Vous suivez activement les évolutions IA et machine learning et réfléchissez à la manière dont elles peuvent renforcer la modélisation stratégique.


Notre manière de travailler


Le développement se fait localement dans VS Code dans un repository Git partagé.
La collaboration se fait via des pull requests dans Azure DevOps.
Le déploiement et l’exécution se font dans Azure Databricks.
Nous travaillons selon une méthode agile, avec un développement itératif, des cycles de feedback courts et une collaboration étroite avec les parties prenantes.
Nous suivons les bonnes pratiques en matière de qualité de code, de gestion de versions et de collaboration.
Vous travaillez dans une équipe où le partage de connaissances, la transparence et la qualité sont essentiels.


Un job fait pour vous ?


Vous avez un master, de préférence en mathématiques, statistiques, data science, sciences de l’ingénieur ou une autre discipline quantitative (ou équivalent par expérience) ET une expérience de minimum 4 ans en tant que data scientist en industrie.


ET vous disposez des compétences suivantes :


Vous êtes doté.e d’une solide connaissance de Python pour le data wrangling, l’analyse de données et la modélisation.
Vous avez de l’expérience avec PySpark et le travail avec de grands volumes de données.
Vous avez de l’expérience dans l’industrialisation d’analyses ou de modèles (par exemple la migration d’Excel vers du code de production).
Vous communiquez aisément, aussi bien en français qu’en anglais et pouvez expliquer clairement des modèles complexes et des hypothèses à des parties prenantes non techniques.
Vous avez un esprit structuré et êtes orienté.e solution et qualité.
Vous avez un intérêt pour le machine learning, les applications d’IA, les données géographiques ou outils GIS (par ex. QGIS, ArcGIS), le transport, la mobilité ou des systèmes opérationnels complexes ? Il s’agit d’atouts en plus.


Si vous avez obtenu votre diplôme à l'étranger, vous devez être en possession d'une attestation d'équivalence délivrée par la Communauté française pour pouvoir participer à la sélection.

Chaque personne est unique. Il est important pour nous que vous puissiez être vous-même. C’est ce qui rend la SNCB plus forte. C’est pourquoi nous accordons beaucoup d’importance à la diversité et à l’inclusion .

Qu'avons-nous à vous offrir ?

Choisir la SNCB, c'est choisir un environnement où vous pouvez apprendre et vous épanouir. Vous serez entouré de chouettes collègues qui vous soutiendront. En tant que Data Scientist, vous recevez un package salarial qui se compose de :


Un salaire attractif complété d'un 13e mois, d'une indemnité de frais, des chèques-repas et d'un bonus collectif et individuel.
Une partie du salaire est offerte sous forme de budget flexible. Vous pouvez opter pour la location d'un vélo, une voiture en leasing, pour le remboursement de l'épargne-pension, etc.
Une assurance hospitalisation et collective.
Un horaire flexible, un bon équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée grâce à 33 jours de congés par an.
Un abonnement de train en Belgique, des avantages sur les lignes internationales et une indemnité vélo.
Des avantages pour votre famille comme des réductions sur les sorties en famille et dans les magasins.


Où allez-vous travailler ?

Vous travaillerez à proximité de la gare du Midi, dans le bâtiment Delta.

What’s next ?


Vous postulez en ligne.
Nous lisons votre CV et votre lettre de motivation avec grande attention. Nous vous appelons pour faire plus ample connaissance.
Vous passez un entretien d’embauche dans lequel vous pourrez montrer votre motivation et vos compétences. Pour préparer cet entretien, vous complétez un questionnaire de personnalité et préparez un business case.
Il s’est bien passé ? Vous passez un deuxième entretien.
Nous vous faisons une proposition de contrat.
Vous commencez votre premier jour de travail !


Nous nous réservons le droit d'ajuster ou de suspendre cette procédure en cas de nécessité. Notre équipe de recrutement sélectionnera les profils les plus en adéquation avec les exigences du poste afin de garantir une correspondance optimale.

Avez-vous encore des questions?

Sur la procédure de sélection :

Amandine Ribaucourt

amandine.ribaucourt@sncb.be

Title: Data Science internship
Description:
In a nutshell

Turn big data into better customer experiences by developing AI-driven solutions that help Toyota detect vehicle quality issues and shape the future of mobility.

Company Information

TOYOTA is one of the world’s largest automobile manufacturers and a leading global corporation. Founded in 1937. Toyota now sells vehicles in 170 countries and employs over 350.000 people. Based in Brussels, Belgium, and staffed by 2.700 people and more than 60 nationalities, Toyota Motor Europe (TME) handles the wholesale marketing of Toyota and Lexus vehicles, parts & accessories, and manages Toyota’s European R&D, manufacturing, and engineering operations.

We are excited to be transforming into a mobility company focusing on bringing even more happiness to our customers through various mobility solutions.

Team/division Description

Your team and your responsibilities

TME Customer Quality’s (CQ) primary role is to resolve quality concerns in the European markets, together with R&D and manufacturing partners. CQ Business Reform team (BR) enables faster issue detection by more efficient data monitoring and analysis of possible quality concerns through digitalisation, automation and system developments.

Your project

As Intern In The CQ Business Reform Team


Support translating Business requirements into a Data Science project which generates valuable insights for the Quality engineers
Establish the data science workflow, starting from the data collection, extraction, exploration and statistical analysis
Develop the models and analytics required for the business insights
Generate value from data by building dashboards or webapps which provide real time insight from your analytics into the Quality process
contribute to the team wiki page by documenting your work


Your Profile


You are fluent in English (TME’s business language)
Student in last year of master study, data science, automotive or computer engineering
Familiar with Data Science & Statistics
Knowledge of Python
Knowledge of SQL
Good understanding of NLP and LLMs
Interest in automotive, software, Big Data, connected cars
Quick and eager learner with a flexible mind and eye for details

Starting date: 6/09/2026

Duration: 6 months

Confidentiality

Due to business requirement, not all performed projects can be reflected in the internship report. This issue needs to be discussed with candidate/school in advance.

It is mandatory that applicants are students for the entire period of the internship.

We can only accept students with an EEA or Swiss citizenship (not requiring a work permit or visa to be eligible to perform an internship in Belgium)

We offer

We offer a dynamic multicultural work environment with broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise.

Your benefits


Flexible working hours and hybrid working (homeworking, 10 days from abroad);
Company restaurant – Warm dishes, salads and desserts daily, including Japanese and traditional Belgian specialties, as well as a vegan/vegetarian option;
Sport facilities – Tennis, squash, volleyball, football and softball pitches are available at various Toyota sites;
Team building activities and other personnel related events;
Programs to support your physical and social well-being.


A truly diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace

At Toyota Motor Europe, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all. All our recruitment decisions are based on the individual and their qualifications along with the job requirements. The selection process is without regard to nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, ethnicity, disability or other personal characteristics. We do not tolerate harassment or discrimination as our individual experiences and overall diversity is our key strength. Together we have the power to make a positive change and go beyond the ordinary.

If you need any specific consideration for the interview process due to disability or any other reason, please let the recruiter know.

 We offer

We offer a dynamic multicultural work environment with broad opportunities to learn every day and exciting career pathways that help you explore different disciplines or areas of expertise.

Your benefits

  • Flexible working hours and hybrid working (homeworking, 10 days from abroad);

  • Programs to support your physical and social well-being.

A truly diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace

At Toyota Motor Europe, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all. All our recruitment decisions are based on the individual and their qualifications along with the job requirements. The selection process is without regard to nationality, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, religion, ethnicity, disability or other personal characteristics. Together we have the power to make a positive change and go beyond the ordinary.

If you need any specific consideration for the interview process due to disability or any other reason, please let the recruiter know.

Title: Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484
Description:
About the job Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI Job ID: JP054484

Data Scientist – Machine Learning & AI

We are seeking a Data Scientist to design, develop, and maintain a modular and future-proof machine learning framework while delivering advanced analytics and predictive modeling solutions to support business objectives.

Key Responsibilities Machine Learning Framework Development


Design and implement a scalable and reusable machine learning framework.
Establish best practices for data preprocessing, model training, validation, deployment, and lifecycle management.
Implement monitoring, feedback loops, and model retraining mechanisms.
Manage model versioning, reproducibility, logging, and system monitoring.
Prepare infrastructure for future cloud migration initiatives.
Document processes, methodologies, and technical solutions.
Coach and support team members in the adoption and use of the framework.
Present technical analyses and results to business stakeholders.


Data Science & Machine Learning Solutions


Translate business requirements into technical solutions using an iterative development approach.
Identify, assess, and validate relevant data sources.
Perform data analysis, data preparation, and exploratory data analysis (EDA).
Develop feature engineering strategies to improve model performance.
Select, evaluate, compare, and optimize machine learning algorithms.
Build, tune, automate, and maintain predictive models.
Continuously evaluate and improve model accuracy and effectiveness.
Communicate insights and recommendations through clear visualizations and presentations.


Required Skills & Experience


Strong experience with Python for Data Science and Machine Learning.
Solid knowledge of SQL and database technologies.
Experience with Machine Learning and AI techniques for data analysis.
Experience developing and evaluating analytical and predictive models.
Hands-on experience with Jupyter Notebook and KNIME.
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric.
Experience with Microsoft DevOps or similar CI/CD and version control platforms.
Experience working with Microsoft SQL databases.
Knowledge of Oracle database environments.
Familiarity with Azure Machine Learning and Azure ML SDK.


Languages


Native proficiency in either Dutch or French.
Ability to read and understand the other national language.


Working Model


Hybrid work environment.
On-site presence required 2 days per week in Brussels, with remote work available on remaining days.


Selection Process


Interview combined with a practical technical assignment.

Title: Data scientist
Description:
Taken en verantwoordelijkheden

Hoe maak jij mee het verschil?

Als Data Scientist – in de Corporate strategy dienst – ontwikkel en onderhoud je strategische modellen die diepgaande inzichten opleveren en direct bijdragen aan beleidskeuzes en investeringsbeslissingen.

Je werkt op het kruispunt van data, business en strategie en zorgt ervoor dat complexe assumpties en berekeningen transparant, robuust en reproduceerbaar worden geïmplementeerd in schaalbare data-oplossingen.

Wat is jouw rol bij NMBS?

Als Data Scientist Heb Je De Volgende Verantwoordelijkheden


Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en industrialiseert strategische modellen op basis van businessassumpties, in samenwerking met de relevante stakeholders.
Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende NMBS-directies in een transversale context over de hele organisatie.
Je vertaalt bestaande Excel-modellen, die vandaag lokaal beheerd worden, naar robuuste en schaalbare PySpark-oplossingen.
Je automatiseert dataverwerking en modelruns via pipelines in Databricks.
Je werkt met Python en PySpark binnen een Azure Databricks-omgeving om data te verwerken, modellen te implementeren en analyses uit te voeren.
Je waakt over transparantie, reproduceerbaarheid en best practices bij het implementeren van modellen.
Je verkent opportuniteiten om bijkomende databronnen en analysetechnieken (zoals ruimtelijke data, AI of geavanceerde visualisaties) te integreren wanneer deze een duidelijke meerwaarde bieden voor strategische inzichten.
Je volgt de AI en machine learning evoluties actief op en denkt mee na over hoe ze het strategisch modelleren kunnen versterken.


Zo werken we


Ontwikkeling gebeurt lokaal in VS Code in een gedeelde Git-repository.
Samenwerking verloopt via pull requests in Azure DevOps.
Deployment en uitvoering gebeuren in Azure Databricks.
We werken volgens een agile manier van werken, met iteratieve ontwikkeling, korte feedbackcycli en nauwe samenwerking met stakeholders.
We volgen best practices rond codekwaliteit, versiebeheer en samenwerking.
Je werkt in een team waar kennisdeling, transparantie en kwaliteit centraal staan.


Waarom is deze job iets voor jou?


Je beschikt over een masterdiploma bij voorkeur in wiskunde, statistiek, data science, ingenieurswetenschappen of een andere kwantitatieve richting) (of gelijkwaardig door ervaring) EN een ervaring van minstens 4 jaar ervaring in de industrie als data scientist.


EN Je Beschikt Over De Volgende Vaardigheden


Je hebt een sterke Python-kennis voor data wrangling data-analyse en modellering.
Je hebt ervaring met PySpark en het werken met grotere datasets.
Je hebt ervaring met het industrialiseren van analyses of modellen (bijvoorbeeld migratie van Excel naar productiecode).
Je communiceert vlot, zowel in het Nederlands als in het Engels en je kan complexe modellen en assumpties helder toelichten aan niet-technische stakeholders.
Je hebt een gestructureerde mindset en bent oplossings- en kwaliteitsgericht.
Heb je interesse in machine learning, AI-toepassingen, geografische data of GIS-tools, transport, mobiliteit of complexe operationele systemen? Die zijn extra troeven.


Indien je jouw diploma in het buitenland hebt behaald, dien je in het bezit te zijn van een gelijkwaardigheidsattest afgeleverd door de Vlaamse Overheid (NARIC-Vlaanderen) om deel te kunnen nemen aan de selectie.

We vinden het dan ook belangrijk dat je jezelf kan zijn. Want dat maakt NMBS sterker. Daarom hechten we veel belang aan diversiteit en inclusie.

Wat mag je van ons verwachten?

Kiezen Voor NMBS Is Kiezen Voor Een Omgeving Waarin Je Kan Bijleren En Groeien. Je Wordt Gesteund En Omringd Door Fijne Collega's. Als Data Scientist Krijg Je Een Loonpakket Dat Bestaat Uit


Een aantrekkelijk salaris aangevuld met een 13e maand, onkostenvergoeding, maaltijdcheques en een collectieve en individuele bonus.
Een deel van het salaris wordt aangeboden als een flexibel budget. Je kan daarbij kiezen voor fietslease, lease auto, terugbetaling pensioensparen, ...
Een hospitalisatie- en groepsverzekering.
Flexibele werkuren, de mogelijkheid om te telewerken en 33 vakantiedagen per jaar.
Een treinabonnement in België, kortingen op internationale treinreizen en een fietsvergoeding.
Voordelen voor je gezin: o.a. kortingen op gezinsactiviteiten en in winkelketens.


Waar ga je werken?

Je werkt in de buurt van het station Brussel-Zuid, in het gebouw Delta.

What’s next?


Je solliciteert online.
We screenen je CV en motivatiebrief. We bellen je eventueel op om kennis te maken en om je kennis van het Frans te bevragen.
We nodigen je uit voor een eerst (online) sollicitatiegesprek waarin je je motivatie en competenties kan tonen. Ter voorbereiding op dit gesprek vragen we jou om een online persoonlijkheidsvragenlijst in te vullen. Ter plaatse, bereid je een business case voor.
Verloopt alles positief? Volgt er nog een tweede gesprek.
We doen je een contractvoorstel.
Je start in je nieuwe job!


Wij behouden ons het recht om deze procedure aan te passen of op te schorten indien nodig. Ons rekruteringsteam zal de profielen selecteren die het best aansluiten bij de functie-eisen om een optimale match te garanderen.

Heb je nog vragen?

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
An exciting opportunity for a skilled Data Scientist to join a dynamic team focused on advancing the field of biopharmaceutical manufacturing. This role will involve collaborating with various functions within the organization to drive innovation and excellence in data science applications related to drug substance manufacturing processes. You will play a crucial role in supporting the development and optimization of key processes and methodologies.

Responsibilities


Provide advanced statistical and data science support across multiple functions, including cell culture, purification, and analytical activities.
Engage in process optimization, investigation, comparability studies, monitoring, method transfer, validation, and characterization.
Contribute to the preparation of protocols and reports with significant statistical content through data collection, experiment design, and data analysis.
Ensure the traceability and correctness of collected data, facilitating timely information exchange with relevant stakeholders.
Maintain compliance with legal standards and regulations, ensuring high-quality scientific and technical documentation.



Technical Skills


Proficient in statistical data analysis and data science techniques.
Experience working in a cGMP environment, with a strong understanding of regulatory guidelines.
Domain knowledge in biologics, particularly in upstream or downstream development and manufacturing.
Ability to lead complex technical projects effectively.
Strong problem-solving skills with a change agent mentality to propose innovative solutions to scientific challenges.



Profile


Holds a Bachelor's degree in data sciences, statistics, or a related discipline with a minimum of 6 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry; or a Master's degree with at least 4 years of experience; or a Ph.D. with a minimum of 2 years of experience.
Demonstrates effective communication skills, both written and verbal, for cross-functional collaboration, particularly in English. Proficiency in Dutch is advantageous.
A team player capable of working autonomously in a collaborative, dynamic environment.



Practical Information


This position is based at a biopharmaceutical manufacturing site near Geel.
Fluent English is required, and proficiency in Dutch is a plus.
This is a long-term opportunity available for both consultants on our payroll and freelance professionals.



Contactperson & Reference

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
ML OPS Engineer / AI Engineer

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international group based in Denmark and Belgium, comprising over 350 consultants dedicated to unlocking the full business potential of companies through technology and digital transformation.

We have a specialized Financial Services practice that is experiencing rapid growth. Over 100 consultants focus exclusively on the financial services sector, assisting banks and insurances in redesigning their operations to meet changing client expectations, adopt new technologies, and adhere to evolving regulations (DORA, RIS, AI Act, AML, KYC, etc).

At NORRIQ, our employees are at the heart of our organization and our success.. We hire and nurture passionate individuals who strive for excellence for both our clients and colleagues. Our culture supports personal and professional growth with training, opportunities, and creative freedom. Regardless of when you join or how long you stay, you'll experience an unforgettable NORRIQ journey and become a part of our extended family. Connected forever!

Role Overview

As an MLOps Engineer / AI Engineer, you will play a central role in bridging Data Science and IT Operations. You will design, deploy, and scale production-grade machine learning and AI solutions for our financial services clients. Your mission is to ensure robust, secure, and efficient AI operations—covering deployment, observability, lifecycle management, and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for someone who loves building AI infrastructure, optimizing performance, and enabling data scientists to move faster and safer.

Key Responsibilities


Deploy ML/AI Models: Work with Data Scientists to deploy secure, scalable, and reliable models into production.
Build CI/CD Pipelines: Automate testing, packaging, and deployment for data and model workflows.
Manage Data Pipelines: Design and maintain efficient data flows across storage systems, databases, and platforms.
Set Up Environments: Manage dependencies, development environments, and tooling for smooth project onboarding.
Operate Kubernetes: Deploy and maintain containerized AI components using Kubernetes.
Monitor & Troubleshoot: Oversee model/application performance, resolve incidents, and conduct root-cause analyses.
Optimize & Innovate: Identify improvements, adopt new technologies, and enhance AI engineering practices.


Requirements

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong background in programming, system engineering, or DevOps is highly valued.


Technical Skills


Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience with REST APIs and Git.
Hands-on expertise in CI/CD and MLOps/DevOps practices.
Practical experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Familiarity with Linux, software testing, and modern software engineering workflows.
Nice to have: SQL/PostgreSQL, MLflow, Helm, or related tooling.


Soft Skills


Collaborative and team-oriented mindset.
Proactive, solution-driven, and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Strong communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.


Languages


Fluent in English (spoken and written).
Knowledge of additional languages (Dutch or French) is a plus.


Why Joining Us?


You will be part of a fast growing international company with a unique people culture
A very competitive salary package including a company car, iPhone and different allowances
You will get extensive trainings in Belgium and Denmark on technical matters, as well as soft skills and project management, and you will have access to new technologies and innovative equipment.
You will join a dynamic and growing team with a great mix of young and experienced professionals focusing on financial services.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer Job ID: JP054754
Description:
About the job Machine Learning Engineer Job ID: JP054754Machine Learning Engineer

We are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to design, build, deploy, and maintain production-ready machine learning solutions that enhance business processes and digital services. You will work closely with data engineers, software developers, architects, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, reliable, and compliant ML solutions across both Azure and on-premises environments.

Key Responsibilities


Prepare, transform, and validate data from multiple sources for machine learning use cases.
Design, train, test, and optimize ML models for classification, regression, forecasting, scoring, and detection.
Develop feature engineering processes and ensure data quality and reproducibility.
Deploy machine learning models into production through APIs, backend services, and batch processes.
Build and maintain ML pipelines, CI/CD workflows, and MLOps processes.
Monitor model performance, data drift, model drift, latency, and system reliability.
Implement version control for code, datasets, configurations, and ML models.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate ML solutions into existing applications.
Document technical implementations and contribute to ML engineering best practices.
Ensure solutions comply with relevant regulations, including the European AI Act.


Required Skills & Experience


Strong experience with Python for data science and machine learning development.
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as Scikit-learn, PyTorch, LangGraph, Ollama, and related open-source tools.
Knowledge of feature engineering, data preparation, model validation, and SQL.
Experience deploying ML components into production applications and backend services.
Familiarity with Docker, CI/CD pipelines, version control, and deployment of ML services.
Experience with MLOps, experiment tracking, model registry, workflow orchestration, and Azure DevOps.
Knowledge of monitoring tools for ML systems, including logging, metrics, drift detection, OpenTelemetry, and Dynatrace.
Experience with Azure and C#/.NET/Blazor is an advantage.


Languages


Native-level Dutch or French.
Good understanding of the second national language.


Work Arrangement


Hybrid role with 2 days onsite per week in Brussels and 3 days remote.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer




The Role

We're seeking a Machine Learning Engineer to design, build, and operate production AI/ML systems that power intelligent automation. This role focuses on taking AI solutions from concept to production, including model fine-tuning, inference pipelines, retrieval systems, and integration with enterprise design platforms.




Key Responsibilities

Build and own end-to-end ML pipelines from data ingestion through training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring.
Fine-tune foundation models and develop domain-specific AI solutions.
Design and implement RAG architectures, embeddings strategies, and vector search solutions.
Develop scalable data pipelines from complex engineering and design datasets.
Deploy, serve, and monitor models in production using best-practice MLOps frameworks.
Collaborate with software engineering teams to integrate ML capabilities into customer-facing applications.




Skills & Experience




Essential

Strong AWS experience, including SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, Step Functions, S3, and DynamoDB.
Proven experience building and maintaining production ML systems.
Expertise in Python, PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, pandas, and NumPy.
Experience with model fine-tuning, evaluation frameworks, CI/CD, monitoring, and drift detection.
Hands-on experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, and embeddings.
Strong software engineering fundamentals, including APIs, Docker, and Infrastructure as Code.




Desirable

Experience with BIM, construction technology, or digital design platforms.
Knowledge of Autodesk Platform Services, Revit, AutoCAD, or Autodesk Construction Cloud.
Experience with generative design, spatial optimisation, graph neural networks, or geometric deep learning.
Familiarity with Dynamo, Grasshopper, or computational design tools.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We're looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our client's team and contribute to innovative, data-driven initiatives that deliver real business impact. In this role, you'll work on a diverse portfolio of projects, applying advanced analytics and AI to solve complex operational challenges. Areas of focus include predictive analytics, security insights, energy optimization, generative AI solutions, and the development of strategic data models that drive smarter decision-making.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and deploy advanced data science solutions using Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Generative AI techniques.
Translate complex business challenges into scalable, data-driven solutions.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, including business, operations, IT, and other technical teams.
Apply best practices throughout the model lifecycle, from development and validation to deployment and monitoring.
Promote the adoption of modern data science methodologies, tools, and industry best practices.



Your Profile


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
At least 5 years of experience in Data Science, with a proven track record of delivering industrial or enterprise-scale analytics solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based platforms for developing and deploying production-ready data analytics and AI solutions.
Strong programming skills and solid knowledge of a broad range of data science, machine learning, and AI techniques.
Demonstrated experience bringing ML and AI models into production environments.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to convert complex business requirements into actionable insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Fluent in English.
Available to work from the client's office two days per week (Tuesday and Thursday).
