Title: Expert in AI/NLP/ML
HTML:
We currently have a vacancy for an Expert in AI/NLP/ML fluent in English, to offer his/her services as an expert who will be based in Brussels, Belgium. The work will be carried out either in the company's premises or on-site at the customer's premises. In the context of the first assignment, the successful candidate will be integrated into the Development team of the company that will closely cooperate with a major client's IT team on-site.

Tasks:

    • Engaging with Business stakeholders, organising workshops and meetings to gather requirements;
    • Analysing needs in the archiving domain and translating them into robust technical specifications;
    • Designing and proposing AI- and cloud-based architectures that are secure, scalable, and costeffective;
    • Developing, testing, and deploying solutions leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), vector databases, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain);
    • Building prototypes, Proofs of Concept, and production-ready services using Python and Jupyter Notebooks;
    • Leveraging AWS Cloud Services (e.g., S3, Lambda, SageMaker, Bedrock, API Gateway, ECS/EKS, CloudFormation/Terraform);
    • Preparing and maintaining technical documentation, ensuring knowledge transfer within the team;
    • Integrating solutions into existing data and archiving systems;
    • Monitoring, optimising, and supporting deployed solutions over time;
    • Collaborating closely with Business, IT Infrastructure, and Security teams to ensure long-term sustainability.



Requirements

    • Master's degree in IT or a relevant discipline, combined with a minimum of 13 years of relevant working experience in IT;
    • At least 3 years of experience in LLMs: fine-tuning, prompting strategies, evaluation, and deployment;
    • At least 2 years of experience in LangChain or equivalent frameworks for LLM orchestration;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Vector databases;
    • At least 2 years of experience in Python, with practical use of Jupyter Notebooks;
    • At least 2 years of experience in AWS services for AI, data, and cloud-native development;
    • At least 1 year of experience in Machine Learning Operations, including CI/CD pipelines and monitoring of AI solutions;
    • At least 1 year of experience in data engineering and archiving best practices;
    • Good knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes and security best practices in AI and cloud-based deployments would be an advantage;
    • Knowledge of DevOps/Infrastructure-as-Code tools beyond AWS (Terraform, Ansible) and data governance frameworks and compliance standards (e.g., GDPR) would be an advantage;
    • AWS Solutions Architect, AWS Machine Learning, AWS DevOps Engineer, TensorFlow Developer or equivalent AI/ML credentials, Kubernetes Administrator (CKA), TOGAF or equivalent enterprise architecture framework certifications would be an asset;
    • Excellent command of the English language.


Benefits

If you are seeking a career in an exciting, dynamic and multicultural international environment with exciting opportunities that will boost your career, please send us your detailed CV in English, quoting reference (19007/09/2025).

We offer a competitive remuneration (either on contract basis or remuneration with full benefits package), based on qualifications and experience. All applications will be treated as confidential.

You may also consider all our other open vacancies by visiting the career section of our web site (www.eurodyn.com) and follow us on Twitter (@EURODYN_Careers) and LinkedIn.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (www.eurodyn.com) is a leading European Software, Information and Communication Technologies company, operating internationally (Athens, Brussels, Luxembourg, Copenhagen, Berlin, Stockholm, London, Nicosia, Valetta, Vienna, Den Haag, Hong Kong, etc.) The company employs over 1000 engineers, IT experts and consultants (around 3% PhD, 36% MSc and 53% BSc). We design and develop software applications using integrated, state-of-the-art technology. Our current IT projects have a value exceeding 300 million EURO. EUROPEAN DYNAMICS is a renowned supplier of IT services to European Union Institutions, international organizations, European Agencies and national government Administrations in 40 countries and 4 continents.

As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, we are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, colour, age, national origin, ethnicity, gender, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or religion.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (ED) adheres to the General Data Protection Regulation principles by applying its Privacy Policy as published in www.eurodyn.com/privacy. By submitting an application to this position and by sharing your personal data with ED, you acknowledge and accept its Policy and authorise ED to process your personal data for the purposes of the company's recruitment opportunities, in line to the Policy.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
We're looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join our client's growing AI team and help design, build, deploy, and maintain production-ready machine learning solutions. In this role, you'll ensure that ML models are not only accurate during experimentation but also reliable, scalable, and maintainable in production across both Microsoft Azure and on-premises environments. Working closely with data engineers, software developers, architects, and business stakeholders, you'll transform machine learning use cases into sustainable technical solutions while supporting compliance with relevant AI regulations.

Key Responsibilities


Prepare, process, and analyse data from multiple internal and external sources to support machine learning initiatives.
Design and implement feature engineering processes while ensuring data quality, consistency, and reproducibility.
Develop, train, validate, and optimise machine learning models for use cases such as classification, regression, forecasting, anomaly detection, and scoring.
Evaluate and benchmark models using appropriate techniques, with a strong focus on performance, explainability, maintainability, and production readiness.
Deploy machine learning models as scalable services, APIs, or batch processes and contribute to standardized deployment practices.
Build and maintain MLOps pipelines, CI/CD processes, and automated deployment workflows for ML solutions.
Monitor model performance, latency, stability, data drift, and model drift, while implementing retraining and feedback mechanisms to ensure long-term effectiveness.
Apply version control to code, models, configurations, and datasets, ensuring reliable and repeatable deployments.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define technical solutions, document implementations, and promote best practices in machine learning engineering, testing, deployment, and monitoring.



Your Profile


Proven experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or in a similar role, delivering production-grade machine learning solutions.
Strong understanding of the complete machine learning lifecycle, from data preparation and model development to deployment and operational support.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure, MLOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and production deployment of machine learning models.
Experience integrating ML models into backend services, APIs, or data processing workflows.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a pragmatic, results-oriented mindset.
Strong focus on software quality, scalability, maintainability, and operational reliability.
Ability to communicate technical concepts effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
A collaborative approach, strong sense of ownership, and eagerness to continuously learn and adopt new technologies and best practices.
Fluent in either Dutch or French, with a good understanding of the second national language.



What's Offered

This is a hybrid position, requiring two days per week on-site at our client's office and allowing remote work for the remaining three days.

Title: Machine Learning Platform Engineer
Description:
Machine Learning Platform Engineer

Remote (European Union) – Company based in Helsinki, Finland

AI Infrastructure | Machine Learning Platforms | Deep Tech




For one of our clients, a fast-growing AI Infrastructure scale-up based in Helsinki, we are looking for a Machine Learning Platform Engineer to help build the cloud platform powering the next generation of production AI systems.




Working alongside machine learning engineers, platform engineers, and software developers, you'll design and operate the infrastructure that makes AI development faster, more reliable, and easier to scale.




If you're passionate about cloud-native engineering, MLOps, automation, and enabling world-class AI teams, this is an opportunity to work at the heart of one of Europe's fastest-growing AI technology companies.




Technical Environment

• Python

• Kubernetes

• Docker

• MLflow

• Kubeflow or KServe

• AWS, Azure, or GCP

• Terraform

• GitOps & CI/CD Pipelines




What You'll Be Working On

• Designing and building scalable machine learning platforms for production environments

• Developing automated model training, deployment, and monitoring pipelines

• Supporting machine learning engineers with reliable self-service infrastructure and tooling

• Building Infrastructure as Code and deployment automation for cloud-native AI platforms

• Improving observability, reliability, and operational performance across ML systems

• Optimising model serving, inference performance, and platform scalability

• Collaborating with software, data, and platform engineers to continuously improve the AI development lifecycle




Experience Required

• 4+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, MLOps, DevOps, Cloud Engineering, or Machine Learning Infrastructure

• Strong Python development and automation skills

• Hands-on experience with Kubernetes and containerised production environments

• Experience supporting machine learning workloads in cloud environments

• Strong understanding of Infrastructure as Code using Terraform or similar tools

• Experience building CI/CD pipelines and cloud-native platforms




Nice to Have

• Experience supporting Large Language Models or Generative AI platforms

• Vector databases and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures

• Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, or Datadog

• Experience with distributed training and high-performance inference systems




Why Join?

🚀 Fully remote role open across the European Union

🚀 Work with cutting-edge cloud-native and machine learning technologies

🚀 High level of ownership with opportunities to influence platform architecture and engineering standards

🚀 Work with cutting-edge cloud-native and machine learning technologies

Title: BE - Data Scientist
Description:
Overview

Who are we?

CBTW is a global tech solutions company.

We empower businesses with tailored tech and business solutions that drive innovation and sustainable growth.

With over 2 500 experts across 21 countries, we deliver results through flexible engagement models like: whether augmenting teams, managing complex projects, or building long-term operations.

Thanks to our 5 service lines, we specialize in:


AI & Data Platforms,
Cloud & Enterprise Platforms,
Cybersecurity,
Software Engineering,
Strategy, Product & Transformation.


More info on our website: https://cbtw.tech/

What service line will you be part of?

As a Data Scientist, you will join our AI & Data Platforms expertise.

AI & Data Platforms is CBTW's service line dedicated to supporting organizations in leveraging their data and fulfilling their AI ambitions to achieve tangible results. We focus on adoption and measurable impact from day one, combining our comprehensive, end-to-end expertise with our ecosystem of leading partners such as SAS, Snowflake, Microsoft and Databricks.

Our clients rely on us to support with:


Data Platform modernisation, migration and optimisation
AI-driven Business Insights & Customer Analytics
Cutting-edge GenAI products & Agentic AI automation
Unified Data Platform & Cloud Infrastructure
Data & AI Governance
BI & Analytics for business teams


Responsibilities

What is your role?

You will be part of a journey where you will have two impacts: a client impact and an internal impact. For the internal impact, based on your skills and time, you will be joining our internal data community at CBTW, to share knowledge and best practices. For the client impact, tasks can vary depending on the client and the project, but as a Data Engineer, you will mainly:


Mining both structured and unstructured data to identify patterns and provide information through predictive models
Explore and collect data from multiple sources to apply or develop analytical ad statistical methods and machine learning models
Apply statistics and machine learning algorithms to discover patterns in datasets


Qualifications

What are we offering?

EVOLUTION:


A career development expert will follow up on your professional path within the company and support you in shaping and growing your career,
Catalogue with a wide variety of trainings for hard and soft skills, private language classes, unlimited access to an E-learning platform (Udemy, Code Academy... ) and possibility to get certified every year,
Training days credits.


COLLABORATION:


You will join a community of [job title] experts where colleagues support each other on projects, share knowledge through workshops and events to keep learning and evolving, and build international connections, while also enjoying great moments and drinks together,
Participate in internal projects to innovate and develop our solutions portfolio,
Join one of our collectives (Wellness, Agile, RSE, Gaming ...) and share your interests with other positive thinkers,
You’ll also enjoy several unforgettable company parties throughout the year. Events that CBTW has truly mastered the art of organizing.


ON TOP OF THAT:


We offer a competitive salary with a flexible mobility package that allows you to choose options such as having mobility benefits (electric cars, electric bikes, public transportation subscription).


Your next adventure starts at CBTW!

Do you see a match? We would love to see your application coming in! 😉

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Role Title: Machine Learning Engineer

Role Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Hourly Rate: $40 - $100/hour




micro1 is engaging Machine Learning Engineers to contribute expertise to a dynamic customer project. In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.




Scope of Work

Design, develop, and refine machine learning models using Python and relevant libraries to address project objectives.
Analyze large datasets and leverage MongoDB to manage and retrieve data efficiently for model training and validation.
Collaborate with cross-functional contributors to identify areas for model improvement and implement robust solutions.
Conduct thorough model evaluation, tuning hyperparameters, and benchmarking results to ensure optimal performance.
Document methodologies, experiments, and outcomes to ensure transparent and repeatable workflows.
Integrate data pipelines and preprocessing workflows to streamline training and inference processes.
Deliver actionable insights and recommendations based on data-driven findings and machine learning outcomes.




Preferred Qualifications

Demonstrated expertise with Python, including deep familiarity with machine learning frameworks such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
Hands-on experience with MongoDB for data manipulation, storage, and retrieval within machine learning projects.
Strong problem-solving skills and a track record of delivering innovative ML solutions in real-world settings.
Understanding of model evaluation metrics, feature engineering, and effective data preprocessing techniques.
Background in deploying or operationalizing machine learning models in cloud or enterprise environments.
Clear written documentation and communication skills for sharing technical findings and best practices.
Ability to adapt quickly to evolving project requirements and contribute collaboratively in a remote setting.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer (LLMs & Generative AI)

Location: Candidates must be based in the UK, Germany, France, or New York (USA).




A company at the intersection of AI and healthcare is looking for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer to design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions. This role focuses on Large Language Models (LLMs), Generative AI, and modern deep learning systems, bridging research and production.




Key responsibilities:

Lead the development of scalable machine learning libraries, tools, and research workflows.
Build infrastructure that enables reproducible experimentation and efficient model development.
Translate research prototypes into production-ready machine learning systems.
Design and optimize training and inference pipelines for large-scale models.
Promote software engineering best practices, including testing, modularity, CI/CD, and containerization.
Collaborate closely with researchers, data scientists, and engineering teams to accelerate AI development.
Mentor engineers and help drive technical excellence across the team.




Required experience:

Master's or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, AI, or a related quantitative field.
5+ years of professional experience developing production machine learning systems in Python.
Strong expertise with PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Deep understanding of Transformers, attention mechanisms, LLMs, Generative AI, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), agentic AI, and the Hugging Face ecosystem.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), MLOps, Docker, and distributed training or inference.
Strong software engineering skills and experience building maintainable, scalable systems.




Desirable experience:

Healthcare, clinical, genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, or medical imaging data.
Open-source contributions or research publications.




If this sounds like a good fit for you or someone in your network, feel free to get in touch for more details.

Title: Freelance Data Scraping Engineer (Python)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

About The Role

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets.
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements.
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior.
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery.
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $37 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

How To Get Started

Simply apply to this post, qualify, and get the chance to contribute to projects that match your technical skills, on your own schedule. From coding and automation to fine-tuning AI outputs, you'll play a key role in advancing AI capabilities and real-world applications.

Requirements


At least 3 year of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required).
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Hands-on experience with LLMs and AI frameworks to enhance automation and problem-solving
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Work fully remote on your own schedule with just a laptop and stable internet connection.
Gain hands-on experience in a unique hybrid environment where human expertise and AI agents collaborate seamlessly — a distinctive skill set in a rapidly growing field.
Participate in performance-based bonus programs that reward high-quality work and consistent delivery
Show more

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
Daarom kies je voor AG




'Supporter van je leven' is niet zomaar een slogan. Het is een ambitie, een mindset. Want bij AG staan mensen centraal. Wij zorgen ervoor dat jouw job zin heeft, zodat jij zin hebt om bij ons te werken. Dag in dag uit maken we samen het verschil voor onze klanten. Zodat ze in alle rust van hun leven kunnen genieten.




Dit is jouw topjob




We zijn op zoek naar een getalenteerde AI Data Scientist met een zakelijke mindset. Je komt terecht in een dynamische omgeving die kansen biedt om analytische vaardigheden toe te passen bij het oplossen van bedrijfsproblemen in zowel België als de rest van de Ageas Groep in andere entiteiten.




Belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden:




Je ontwerpt, ontwikkelt en implementeert data- en AI-oplossingen voor zakelijke behoeften, aan de hand van zowel interne als externe gegevensbronnen.
Je identificeert en communiceert inzichten uit gegevensanalyse om besluitvorming en probleemoplossing te ondersteunen.
Je werkt samen met cross-functionele teams om problemen met gegevens te identificeren en op te lossen.
Je controleert voortdurend AI-modellen op nauwkeurigheid en identificeert en verhelpt proactief verschilen in gegevens.




Herken je jezelf hierin?




Je bezit een masterdiploma in een kwantitatief vakgebied (bijv. statistiek, wiskunde, informatica, economie, bedrijfskunde) of een verwant vakgebied met een sterke focus op gegevensanalyse.
Je hebt minstens vijf jaar ervaring met gegevensanalyse, bij voorkeur in een bedrijfsomgeving.
Je hebt ervaring met AI & GenAI, computervisie, NLP en/of deep learning.
Je hebt een sterke kennis van gegevensanalysetechnieken en -tools, zoals Python, SQL, PBI, DataBricks.
Je hebt een sterk zakelijk inzicht en je bent in staat om zakelijke behoeften en vereisten te begrijpen en te communiceren.
Je hebt uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, en kan complexe gegevens op een duidelijke en beknopte manier voorstellen. Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van het Nederlands, Frans en Engels.
Je kan zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband werken, meerdere prioriteiten beheren en strakke deadlines halen.
Je bent bereid om af en toe te reizen en projecten in andere entiteiten aan te nemen.




Dit zijn jouw voordelen




Je krijgt een loonpakket met heel wat voordelen. Een deel daarvan kan je zelf samenstellen. Een leasewagen, extra verlof of verzekeringen? Jij kiest.
Jouw welzijn staat voorop. Daarom zet AG in op een sportief en inspirerend welzijnsprogramma. Toegankelijk voor iedereen.
Flexibele werkuren? Thuiswerken tot drie dagen per week? Bij AG kan het.
Je krijgt de kans om jezelf voortdurend te ontwikkelen. Met een breed aanbod aan opleidingen leer je voortdurend vaardigheden bij. Zo geef je jezelf, en je carrière een boost.
Binnen AG veranderen van functie? En dan zo je comfortzone uitbreiden? Ook dat kan. Jouw carrière, jouw keuze.
Je komt terecht in een dynamische, duurzame werkomgeving. Waar de kantoren ruimte geven om slim samen te werken. En om je volledig te kunnen geven




Ga het avontuur aan




Helemaal verkocht? Druk op de knop en solliciteer vandaag nog. Heb je ondersteuning nodig voor of tijdens het selectieproces? We helpen je graag.

Show more

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!




At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. But this job description is for Data Scientists out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it🪄




As a Data Scientist, your mission would include

Leading the technical aspects of data science projects, from conception to deployment.
Designing and implementing scalable data science solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing infrastructures.
Developing and optimizing machine learning models to drive actionable insights and automation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data engineers and business stakeholders, to align technical solutions with strategic goals.
Taking ownership of projects, ensuring high-quality deliverables in an agile environment.
Providing technical leadership and mentorship to junior data scientists.
Engaging in proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scaling them into production-ready solutions.
Working in cloud-based environments and following MLOps best practices.
Communicating insights and technical solutions effectively to diverse audiences.




You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:




Background

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, or a related technical field.
5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or AI-driven projects.
Experience in diverse industries and solutions, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving skills is considered as a plus.

Technical Skills

Strong programming skills in Python and SQL.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies and cloud platforms (AWS preferred; Azure is a plus).
Experience deploying ML models and following MLOps best practices.
Understanding of data engineering principles to support scalable AI solutions.
Ability to design and implement proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scale them into production.

Soft Skills

Autonomous & Proactive: You take initiative and drive projects forward
Curious & Adaptive: You embrace learning and thrive in dynamic environments.
Solution-Oriented: You balance technical rigor with business impact.
Clear Communicator: You simplify complex ideas for diverse audiences.
Collaborative & Supportive: You work well in teams.
Creative Thinker: You find innovative solutions to challenging problems

Practical Info

Languages: Fluent in English. French or Dutch Native.




We know that finding the right job isn’t just about the role, it’s about what’s in it for you, too. While we’re a start-up, we’re big on taking care of our people right from day one.




Here’s a sneak peek at what we offer:

💰 A compensation package that keeps you motivated

Competitive salary
Mobility budget or company car with a fuel card
30 days of paid time off
A top-tier laptop & smartphone with a subscription
Representation allowances
Hospitalization, group & dental insurance
Meal vouchers & eco-vouchers
Opportunity to earn company stock over time

Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!


At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.


On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.


We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. But this job description is for Data Scientists out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it🪄


As a Data Scientist, your mission would include

  • Leading the technical aspects of data science projects, from conception to deployment.
  • Designing and implementing scalable data science solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing infrastructures.
  • Developing and optimizing machine learning models to drive actionable insights and automation.
  • Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data engineers and business stakeholders, to align technical solutions with strategic goals.
  • Taking ownership of projects, ensuring high-quality deliverables in an agile environment.
  • Providing technical leadership and mentorship to junior data scientists.
  • Engaging in proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scaling them into production-ready solutions.
  • Working in cloud-based environments and following MLOps best practices.
  • Communicating insights and technical solutions effectively to diverse audiences.


You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:


Background

  • Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, or a related technical field.
  • 5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or AI-driven projects.
  • Experience in diverse industries and solutions, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving skills is considered as a plus.

Technical Skills

  • Strong programming skills in Python and SQL.
  • Expertise in machine learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn).
  • Hands-on experience with big data technologies and cloud platforms (AWS preferred; Azure is a plus).
  • Experience deploying ML models and following MLOps best practices.
  • Understanding of data engineering principles to support scalable AI solutions.
  • Ability to design and implement proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scale them into production.

Soft Skills

  • Autonomous & Proactive: You take initiative and drive projects forward
  • Curious & Adaptive: You embrace learning and thrive in dynamic environments.
  • Solution-Oriented: You balance technical rigor with business impact.
  • Clear Communicator: You simplify complex ideas for diverse audiences.
  • Collaborative & Supportive: You work well in teams.
  • Creative Thinker: You find innovative solutions to challenging problems

Practical Info

  • Languages: Fluent in English. French or Dutch Native.

Title: Data scientist
Description:
Hoe maak jij mee het verschil?

Als Data Scientist – in de Corporate strategy dienst – ontwikkel en onderhoud je strategische modellen die diepgaande inzichten opleveren en direct bijdragen aan beleidskeuzes en investeringsbeslissingen.

Je werkt op het kruispunt van data, business en strategie en zorgt ervoor dat complexe assumpties en berekeningen transparant, robuust en reproduceerbaar worden geïmplementeerd in schaalbare data‑oplossingen.

Wat is jouw rol bij NMBS?

Als Data Scientist heb je de volgende verantwoordelijkheden:


Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en industrialiseert strategische modellen op basis van businessassumpties, in samenwerking met de relevante stakeholders.
Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende NMBS-directies in een transversale context over de hele organisatie.
Je vertaalt bestaande Excel‑modellen, die vandaag lokaal beheerd worden, naar robuuste en schaalbare PySpark‑oplossingen.
Je automatiseert dataverwerking en modelruns via pipelines in Databricks.
Je werkt met Python en PySpark binnen een Azure Databricks‑omgeving om data te verwerken, modellen te implementeren en analyses uit te voeren.
Je waakt over transparantie, reproduceerbaarheid en best practices bij het implementeren van modellen.
Je verkent opportuniteiten om bijkomende databronnen en analysetechnieken (zoals ruimtelijke data, AI of geavanceerde visualisaties) te integreren wanneer deze een duidelijke meerwaarde bieden voor strategische inzichten.
Je volgt de AI en machine learning evoluties actief op en denkt mee na over hoe ze het strategisch modelleren kunnen versterken.


Zo werken we


Ontwikkeling gebeurt lokaal in VS Code in een gedeelde Git‑repository.
Samenwerking verloopt via pull requests in Azure DevOps.
Deployment en uitvoering gebeuren in Azure Databricks.
We werken volgens een agile manier van werken, met iteratieve ontwikkeling, korte feedbackcycli en nauwe samenwerking met stakeholders.
We volgen best practices rond codekwaliteit, versiebeheer en samenwerking.
Je werkt in een team waar kennisdeling, transparantie en kwaliteit centraal staan.


Waarom is deze job iets voor jou?


Je beschikt over een masterdiploma bij voorkeur in wiskunde, statistiek, data science, ingenieurswetenschappen of een andere kwantitatieve richting) (of gelijkwaardig door ervaring) EN een ervaring van minstens 4 jaar ervaring in de industrie als data scientist.


EN je beschikt over de volgende vaardigheden:


Je hebt een sterke Python‑kennis voor data wrangling data‑analyse en modellering.
Je hebt ervaring met PySpark en het werken met grotere datasets.
Je hebt ervaring met het industrialiseren van analyses of modellen (bijvoorbeeld migratie van Excel naar productiecode).
Je communiceert vlot, zowel in het Nederlands als in het Engels en je kan complexe modellen en assumpties helder toelichten aan niet‑technische stakeholders.
Je hebt een gestructureerde mindset en bent oplossings- en kwaliteitsgericht.
Heb je interesse in machine learning, AI-toepassingen, geografische data of GIS-tools, transport, mobiliteit of complexe operationele systemen? Die zijn extra troeven.


Indien je jouw diploma in het buitenland hebt behaald, dien je in het bezit te zijn van een gelijkwaardigheidsattest afgeleverd door de Vlaamse Overheid (NARIC-Vlaanderen) om deel te kunnen nemen aan de selectie.

We vinden het dan ook belangrijk dat je jezelf kan zijn. Want dat maakt NMBS sterker. Daarom hechten we veel belang aan diversiteit en inclusie .

Wat mag je van ons verwachten?

Kiezen voor NMBS is kiezen voor een omgeving waarin je kan bijleren en groeien. Je wordt gesteund en omringd door fijne collega's. Als Data Scientist krijg je een loonpakket dat bestaat uit:


Een aantrekkelijk salaris aangevuld met een 13e maand, onkostenvergoeding, maaltijdcheques en een collectieve en individuele bonus.
Een deel van het salaris wordt aangeboden als een flexibel budget. Je kan daarbij kiezen voor fietslease, lease auto, terugbetaling pensioensparen, ...
Een hospitalisatie- en groepsverzekering.
Flexibele werkuren, de mogelijkheid om te telewerken en 33 vakantiedagen per jaar.
Een treinabonnement in België, kortingen op internationale treinreizen en een fietsvergoeding.
Voordelen voor je gezin: o.a. kortingen op gezinsactiviteiten en in winkelketens.


Waar ga je werken?

Je werkt in de buurt van het station Brussel-Zuid, in het gebouw Delta.

What’s next?


Je solliciteert online.
We screenen je CV en motivatiebrief. We bellen je eventueel op om kennis te maken en om je kennis van het Frans te bevragen.
We nodigen je uit voor een eerst (online) sollicitatiegesprek waarin je je motivatie en competenties kan tonen. Ter voorbereiding op dit gesprek vragen we jou om een online persoonlijkheidsvragenlijst in te vullen. Ter plaatse, bereid je een business case voor.
Verloopt alles positief? Volgt er nog een tweede gesprek.
We doen je een contractvoorstel.
Je start in je nieuwe job!


Wij behouden ons het recht om deze procedure aan te passen of op te schorten indien nodig. Ons rekruteringsteam zal de profielen selecteren die het best aansluiten bij de functie-eisen om een optimale match te garanderen.

Heb je nog vragen?

Over de selectieprocedure :

Amandine Ribaucourt

amandine.ribaucourt@sncb.be

Title: Lead Data Scientist (Associate Director)
Description:
Lead Data Scientist (Associate Director):

About the Role:

We are looking for an experienced and forward-thinking Lead Data Scientist to drive impactful data science and AI initiatives. This role blends strategic leadership with hands-on delivery, offering the opportunity to shape innovative solutions while remaining deeply involved in execution.

Working closely with senior stakeholders, you will help define, prioritise, and deliver high-value AI and data science projects that align with business goals. You will also play a key role in building and mentoring a high-performing team while championing best practices and innovation across the function.

Key Responsibilities:


Lead the end-to-end development and delivery of AI and data science projects, ensuring alignment with business priorities
Partner with stakeholders to scope, prioritise, and advance initiatives through structured processes such as an AI incubator
Manage and mentor a small team of data scientists (internal and third-party), supporting both their technical and professional growth
Take ownership of solution adoption, including production handovers and ongoing model performance monitoring
Design and implement forward-looking AI proof-of-concepts, contributing hands-on to development where needed
Drive innovation and best practices in machine learning, analytics, and data science delivery


About You:


Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related field
Extensive experience leading data science and AI initiatives in complex environments
Deep expertise in machine learning, statistical modelling, and business analytics
Strong leadership, stakeholder management, and communication skills, with the ability to influence across all levels
Experience within FMCG or a similar fast-paced industry is highly advantageous
Advanced proficiency in Python, SQL, and cloud platforms such as Azure or Databricks


Why Join Us?


Take ownership of high-impact, business-critical AI initiatives
Work at the intersection of strategy and hands-on delivery
Lead and shape a growing data science capability
Make a measurable difference by delivering solutions that drive real business value

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Senior Data Scientist (Remote, EU) – €60–70k

Permanent




We’re hiring for a Senior Data Scientist to join a PE-backed European tech group that’s in the middle of a major data transformation.




They operate across multiple brands and markets, sitting on a large but historically fragmented data estate. The platform is now being centralised (Databricks on Azure), and the focus is shifting from infrastructure → actual business impact.




This role sits right in that shift.




The role




This isn’t a research-heavy or isolated modelling role.




They need someone who can take real business problems and turn them into solutions that actually get used.




That means:

Building predictive models across customer, product and commercial data
Turning messy, distributed data into clear recommendations
Working directly with stakeholders to define what should be built
Plugging into a growing data platform and shaping how data science is applied across the business




You’ll be part of a small, senior team with strong backing internally. High visibility, high expectations.




What matters

5+ years in data science with real business application
Strong Python and SQL
Proven experience building and deploying models (not just notebooks)
Comfortable with ambiguity and messy data
Able to explain work clearly to non-technical stakeholders




Nice to have:

Experience with churn, customer modelling or commercial use cases
Exposure to cloud environments (Azure, AWS etc.)
Worked in multi-product or multi-brand environments




Context

PE-backed, high growth environment
Multi-brand, pan-European setup
Moving toward a centralised Databricks platform
Big internal push on analytics and AI




Why this role exists

The platform is being built.

Now they need people who can actually use it to drive decisions.




Details

€60–70k base (country dependent)
Fully remote across Europe
Permanent

Title: Data & AI Science Consultant
Description:
Are you willing to make a step in your career and deliver Analytics or AI products from early discovery stages up to industrialized solutions to re-imagine the future of the industry as part of large transformation projects?

If this is the case, this role will be of interest to you!

Team



Be at the forefront of the industry, you’ll create, own and make it a reality for clients looking to better serve their connected customers and operate always-on enterprises.

Become an integral part of our Data& AI team with the credibility, expertise and insight clients depend on.

Be working with famous brands and household names – no worrying about how to explain what you do to your family again!

Work alongside global teams to collaborate and deliver the best solutions for our clients.

Align with our industry partners to develop your career and the solutions we deliver for our clients.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Process



Drive and implement real world solutions into production that drive business value to our clients

Solve challenging business problems using advanced machine learning methods such as Deep Learning and quantitative analytics.

Understand business requirements and support the development of business cases.

Run discovery analytics to identify new and innovative opportunities.

Partner with developers and engineers to deploy machine learning algorithms to deliver business value.

Define approaches to embed and scale machine learning models.

Build reusable assets, solutions and develop best practices for current and future business problems.

Consult on complex analyses and advanced machine learning methods.

Communicate and provide guidance to senior client leadership and teams.



Data



Contribute data science expertise to new sales activities.

Shape DS Strategy and the delivery of DS solutions.

Operate across multiple industries and functions.

Develop others by bringing your unique knowledge and skillsets to the wider practice.



Your team

The Data and AI revolution is changing everything. It’s everywhere – transforming how we work and play. Join Accenture and help transform leading organisations and communities around the world. Accenture Data and AI Science is driving these exciting changes and bringing them to life across 40 industries in more than 120 countries. The sheer scale of our capabilities and client engagements and the way we collaborate with the ecosystem, operate and deliver value provides an unparalleled opportunity to grow and advance.

Accenture’s Data and AI practice covers the range of Data and AI skills, from Strategy, Data Science, Data Architecture, AI Engineering and Visual Insights. When combined with Accenture’s broader Strategy and Consulting practice, we are able to bring together the unique ability to drive end to end business change through the application of Data and AI.

You'll learn, grow and advance in an innovative culture that thrives on shared success, diverse ways of thinking and enables boundaryless opportunities that can drive your career in new and exciting ways.

If you’re looking for a challenging career working in a vibrant environment with access to training and a global network of experts, this could be the role for you. As part of our global team, you'll be working with cutting-edge technologies and will have the opportunity to develop a wide range of new skills on the job.

Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services. With our thought leadership and culture of innovation, we apply industry expertise, diverse skill sets and next-generation technology to each business challenge.

We believe in inclusion and diversity and supporting the whole person. Our core values comprise of Stewardship, Best People, Client Value Creation, One Global Network, Respect for the Individual and Integrity. Year after year, Accenture is recognized worldwide not just for business performance but for inclusion and diversity too.

“Across the globe, one thing is universally true of the people of Accenture: We care deeply about what we do and the impact we have with our clients and with the communities in which we work and live. It is personal to all of us.” – Julie Sweet, Accenture CEO

Your background



Relevant work experience in data science, machine learning, and business analytics

Practical experience in coding languages eg. Python, R, Scala, etc.; (Python preferred)

Proficiency in database technologies eg. SQL, ETL, No-SQL, DW, and Big Data technologies e.g. pySpark, Hive, etc.

Experienced working with structured and also unstructured data eg. Text, PDFs, jpgs, call recordings, video, etc.

Knowledge of machine learning modelling techniques and how to fine-tune those models eg. XGBoost, Deep Neural Networks, Transformers, Markov chains, etc.

Experience using specialized machine learning libraries e.g. Fastai, Keras, Tensorflow, pytorch, sci-kit learn, huggingface, etc.

Title: Data Scientist Engineer
Description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

Are you passionate about data-driven platforms, machine learning models, and innovative applications? As a Data Scientist/Engineer at Devoteam, you'll play a hands-on role in delivering impactful solutions across various clients and industries. Each mission we undertake has clear deliverables and deadlines, ensuring you gain diverse experience. For example, one month you might develop a predictive maintenance model, and the next, a customer segmentation model for a retail client.

Key Responsibilities:


Platform Setup: Design and implement robust data platforms using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud and Databricks.
Data Management: Gather, prepare, and manage data for analysis.
Model Research: Investigate and select appropriate machine learning models.
ML Modeling: Develop and refine machine learning models.
Client Collaboration: Spend approximately 50% of your time working directly with clients, either independently or as part of a team.



Your Role:


Build and maintain data platforms and pipelines using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud, Databricks, and Python.
Develop predictive and machine learning models tailored to client needs.
Advise clients on translating their business requirements into technical solutions using machine learning techniques.
Stay updated on the latest trends in machine learning and AI, sharing insights with clients.
Act as a strategic partner to client Data Science teams, helping them navigate their challenges and aspirations.
Inspire clients with new data-driven opportunities.
Bring best practices in Git, DevOps, and CICD to enhance client data platforms.
Collaborate with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and Business Stakeholders.
Represent Devoteam both online and offline, showcasing our expertise.



Qualifications

About You:


Reside in Belgium. (Mandatory)
Hold a university degree in Computer Science, (Business) Engineering, or a specialized master's in Machine Learning or AI.
Minimum 3 years experience in a similar role.
Have a solid understanding of various machine learning models (e.g., SVM, RNN, Deep Learning) and their applications.
Possess foundational knowledge of Data Science, including sampling techniques and evaluation metrics.
Proficient in SQL and Python.
Experience with Spark and/or cloud ecosystems is a must.
Experience with Databricks is real asset
Eager to learn and passionate about data and machine learning technologies.
Excited to join a dynamic and growing team.
Fluent in English; proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer | Python | PyTorch | Machine Learning | Large Language Models | RAG | Remote, UK and EU
Description:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer | Python | PyTorch | Machine Learning | Large Language Models | RAG | Remote, UK and EU




Summary of the Role:

As a Senior ML Engineer, you'll be the technical leader driving machine learning infrastructure from experimentation to production, ensuring AI-powered solutions deliver measurable impact for customers worldwide. This is a unique opportunity to join as one of the early engineering team members of a well-funded startup building breakthrough applications of large language models (LLMs) and AI agents.




You'll take full ownership of evaluation frameworks, production ML pipelines, and cross-team ML integration, working closely with company leadership and product teams to transform cutting-edge AI research into robust, scalable solutions. Your success will be measured by agent performance improvements and product innovation impact, not just technical metrics. This role is ideal for a hands-on ML engineer who has scaled production ML systems, thinks like a product builder, and wants to drive the productionization of LLMs and ML to solve real-world problems.




Your Contributions:

Build Production-Grade Evaluation Systems: Design and implement evaluation frameworks that measure performance, track improvements, and ensure consistent value delivery.
Drive Experimentation-to-Production Pipeline: Own the ML lifecycle from prototype to production, enabling rapid iteration while maintaining reliability.
Enable Cross-Team ML Integration: Collaborate with product teams to integrate ML into customer-facing features.
Optimize AI Agent Performance: Improve systems through experimentation, prompt engineering, and architecture enhancements.
Scale ML Infrastructure: Develop foundational systems, monitoring, and tooling to support rapid growth.
Partner with Leadership: Work closely with senior leadership while operating with high autonomy.
Mentor Through Excellence: Provide guidance and mentorship to junior ML engineers.




What You Need to Be Successful:

Production ML Experience: 5+ years building and scaling ML systems in production.
Neural Networks Foundation: Strong background in classical and deep learning before specializing in LLMs and transformers.
Product-Focused Mindset: Track record of integrating ML systems into real products.
Multi-Company Perspective: Experience across startups and/or scale-ups.
Technical Versatility: Strong Python skills and adaptability across frameworks and tools (e.g., LangChain, workflow orchestration).
Self-Directed Leadership: Ability to operate autonomously while aligned with leadership.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Experience translating technical capabilities into business value.




Nice to Haves:

Experience with AI agents, LLMs, or generative AI applications
Domain knowledge in cybersecurity or related fields
Background at ML-first companies
Experience with modern MLOps and cloud ML infrastructure
Track record of optimizing model performance and costs




Why Join:

Real-World AI Impact: Apply ML to solve significant industry challenges.
Technical Leadership: Shape infrastructure and systems that will scale.
Expert Team Partnership: Collaborate with experienced professionals from top tech companies and scale-ups.
Build the AI-Native Future: Establish ML practices and standards in a rapidly evolving field.
Multiple Growth Pathways: Opportunities for leadership, technical specialization, or senior IC roles.
Breakthrough Technology: Work at the intersection of generative AI and practical applications.




Senior Machine Learning Engineer | Python | PyTorch | Machine Learning | Large Language Models | RAG | Remote, UK and EU

Title: AI OPS Engineer (✨ immediate start ✨)
Description:
This is a Senior Platform Engineering role with an AI infrastructure focus — not an ML or data science role.







About Us




We are a venture builder creating purpose-driven, evidence-led startups.




We work hands-on with ventures from early validation through scale-up, providing shared infrastructure, operational excellence, and execution discipline — while keeping ventures autonomous and founder-led.




Our model is decentralized and pragmatic:




flat hierarchies
strong ownership
decisions grounded in data







The venture builder exists to accelerate execution, not to centralize power or slow teams down.




Most companies are running AI like it's 2023 — ad hoc tools, no cost attribution, no safety guarantees. We're building the infrastructure layer that changes that.

As our AI Ops Engineer, you'll own the platform that makes AI productive, safe, and economically rational across every team — engineering, product, and operations. This isn't tool configuration. It's building the operating layer that determines how fast our company can learn, ship, and compound.




Why this role exists




AI-assisted development has crossed the threshold from experiment to production system. But most companies are still running AI like it's 2023: ad hoc tool adoption, no cost attribution, no safety guarantees, no cross-team consistency. That's not a tooling gap — it's an infrastructure gap.

The AI Ops Engineer closes it.

You'll own the platform that makes AI productive, safe, and economically rational across every team — engineering, product, operations, and beyond. You're not configuring tools. You're building the operating layer that determines how fast this company can learn, ship, and compound.

If a Context Engineer makes AI effective within a codebase, the AI Ops Engineer makes AI effective everywhere — across repos, teams, workflows, and business functions.

This is one of the highest-leverage roles in the company.




What you'll own




AI-native CI/CD with quality, safety, and intelligence gates
Cross-repo agent infrastructure (Claude Code, MCP servers, shared context libraries)
Multi-model routing strategy and provider management
Token spend visibility and cost-per-outcome optimization
Internal automation workflows (n8n and beyond)
Security scanning and AI-specific risk management
Safe AI enablement for non-technical teams







You're likely a fit if




You automate your own workflows unprompted, have strong opinions about pipeline architecture, and get genuine satisfaction from systems that quietly work at scale.







Core background




DevOps, Platform Engineering, or Developer Experience — with deep CI/CD fluency, strong scripting ability, and an observability mindset. LLM API experience and familiarity with agent tooling are a strong plus.




This role reports to the VP of Engineering / CTO and has direct ownership of AI tooling spend. Compensation is benchmarked against senior platform engineering roles.

We're not hiring someone to keep the lights on. We're hiring someone to build the power plant.







Compensation philosophy




This role sits at the intersection of infrastructure, AI, and company-wide leverage. Compensation reflects that — it's benchmarked against senior platform engineering roles, not junior DevOps.







The mindset we're hiring for




This is not "keep the lights on." This is build the power plant — then make it reliable, safe, observable, scalable, and intelligent.




You'll be trusted with systems that influence how code gets written, reviewed, and shipped. How risk is controlled. How costs are managed. How quickly the entire company can learn, adapt, and execute.




Every team's ceiling is partly determined by the platform you build. Every dollar of AI spend either compounds or evaporates based on the infrastructure you design.




If that sounds exciting — if you want to build the platform that makes everyone else sharper, faster, and more capable — this role is for you.







Please make sure to submit the following assignment to be considered for this role:




https://hiring-agent-sand.vercel.app/apply/ai-ops-engineer




Ideawise is an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Data Scientist - GenAI
Description:
Join Artefact Belgium: Shape the Future of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

Are you an early-career professional excited to harness the power of generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)? Artefact Belgium is seeking a Data Scientist - Gen AI to join our innovative team in Brussels. Our Belgian team specializes in LLM and GenAI projects, and this is your chance to work on cutting-edge solutions, collaborate with top-tier experts, and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic, forward-thinking environment.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Motivated to apply your technical expertise to real-world challenges in AI. Able to thrive in collaborative settings while independently driving your own workstreams. Eager to learn, grow, and push the boundaries of what's possible in AI innovation.

Who We Are

Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. At Artefact, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, with a strong commitment to inclusivity and employee growth.

What You Will Do


Collaborate with the team to design, train, and fine-tune state-of-the-art models for client projects and internal tools.
Develop and integrate agentic AI solutions using tool calling, and emerging protocols like Model Context Protocol (MCP) to create autonomous, context-aware applications.
Research and implement innovative generative AI applications to solve complex business problems.
Design and implement comprehensive evaluation frameworks to measure LLM performance, establishing quality metrics and benchmarks that guide development decisions and ensure reliable, production-ready solutions.
Optimize the performance, scalability, and usability of LLM-powered products and tools.
Contribute to the deployment and maintenance of AI systems, including models, APIs, and supporting infrastructure.
Participate in technical discussions and brainstorming sessions to shape impactful solutions.


What You Bring


A degree in computer science, mathematics, engineering, physics, or a related field.
Experience working with LLMs, generative AI, or advanced NLP applications (e.g., fine-tuning models, using frameworks like Hugging Face or OpenAI).
Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with AI and NLP libraries.
Analytical and problem-solving capabilities, with an ability to address real-world challenges through AI.
Good communication skills in English, Dutch or French; additional languages are a plus.
A growth mindset, adaptability, and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, cutting-edge environment.


What We Offer


A competitive salary with benefits tailored for early-career professionals.
Hands-on experience with state-of-the-art LLM technologies in impactful projects.
A dynamic and supportive work environment that fosters creativity and professional development.

Who You Are

A recent graduate or professional with 1 to 3 years of experience, with hands-on experience working with LLMs and a passion for generative AI and NLP. Artefact is a leading Data & AI consulting firm operating at the forefront of AI innovation. With a global presence, we help clients across industries—public health, manufacturing, etc. —leverage generative AI to achieve transformative outcomes. Our team in Belgium is specialized in LLM and GenAI projects, working on the most advanced applications of these technologies. Title: Python(Machine leaning) QA Lead - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Python(Machine learning) Quality Assurance Lead

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

About This Role

In this hourly, remote contractor role, you will work as a Python(Machine learning) Quality Assurance Lead to oversee quality, consistency, and trainer performance across Python machine learning AI training projects. You will review AI-generated Python code, ML workflows, model explanations, and trainer/QA work; evaluate output quality against project guidelines; provide precise written feedback; and ensure contributors follow expected quality standards. You will assess work for code correctness, machine learning methodology, statistical validity, reproducibility, model-evaluation quality, data leakage risks, package usage, debugging accuracy, readability, maintainability, formatting, instruction-following, and adherence to project-specific rubrics. This role requires strong Python and ML expertise, English communication skills, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to manage quality workflows across remote technical teams. This role is a fast-growing AI Data Services company delivering training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation-model labs. Your Python ML quality leadership will help ensure training data is accurate, executable, statistically sound, reproducible, clearly explained, and aligned with client expectations. Selection process involves an AI interview, a domain-specific task, and an interview with a recruiter. Important: There is no immediate project for this role; however, if qualified, you will be among the first experts we reach out to when relevant opportunities arise. This will also provide you with access to future projects available through our expert network.

Your Profile


Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, or a closely related quantitative field.
Strong grasp of English to follow guidelines, communicate with teams, and provide clear technical feedback.
3+ years of professional experience in Python development, machine learning, data science, ML engineering, model evaluation, research engineering, technical review, or ML education.
Strong understanding of Python fundamentals such as data structures, functions, classes, iterators, comprehensions, exception handling, virtual environments, package management, testing, and debugging.
Strong understanding of ML topics such as supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, train/test splits, cross-validation, model selection, data leakage, regression, classification, clustering, metrics, bias/variance, regularization, and reproducibility.
Ability to evaluate ML content against detailed rubrics and identify issues such as flawed methodology, wrong metrics, data leakage, non-reproducible code, invalid assumptions, hallucinated APIs, misleading conclusions, or incomplete explanations.
Familiarity with NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, XGBoost/LightGBM, Jupyter, matplotlib, seaborn, MLflow, Hugging Face, SQL, GitHub, Docker, and CI/CD is preferred.
Experience leading or supporting remote teams of trainers, annotators, reviewers, engineers, data scientists, ML researchers, coding mentors, or QAs is strongly preferred.
Comfortable working in fast-moving remote environments using Discord, Google Sheets, Google Docs, trackers, dashboards, GitHub, and project management systems.
Highly organized and able to maintain style guides, trackers, FAQs, onboarding materials, honeypots, calibration tasks, and quality documentation.
Experience with AI training, data annotation, LLM evaluation, code QA, ML QA, or rubric-based technical review is a strong plus.


Key Responsibilities


Quality monitoring: Spot-check Python ML items, identify quality issues, provide feedback through DMs, and escalate recurring or critical issues.
Code and ML review: Evaluate AI-generated Python code, ML pipelines, data-preprocessing steps, model training workflows, evaluation logic, debugging responses, and explanations for correctness and reproducibility.
Trainer and QA communication: Update contributors on Discord about guideline changes, workflow updates, and Python/ML-specific review standards.
Question handling: Respond to questions around Python syntax, package usage, data leakage, model validation, metrics, statistical assumptions, reproducibility, notebooks, and rubric interpretation.
Trainer/QA activation management: DM inactive contributors, encourage activation, track follow-ups, and flag availability issues.
Documentation: Create and maintain Python ML style guides, trackers, FAQs, examples, honeypots, calibration tasks, and onboarding materials.
Onboarding and training: Run onboarding/training calls for Python ML contributors.
Risk review: Flag misleading, overconfident, statistically invalid, non-reproducible, insecure, or non-production-ready Python ML recommendations.
Process improvement: Identify recurring quality gaps and build scalable QA processes.

Title: Consultant in Data Science
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



As a consultant in Data Science, you will be involved in transformation projects at our clients. You will execute project assignments in modelling and digital transformation (business/IT analysis and partial coding) environments with a good deal of business perspectives:



Machine learning, AI and statistics: developing predictive models of decision making (supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, econometrics, forecasting, quantitative diagnosis,...)
Operational research : assistance in the design and implementation of constraint optimization solutions and simulation models
Big Data : Algorithmic knowledge for large and unstructured data processing

You will get the opportunity to actively contribute to Sia's internal activities:



Development and reinforcement of our offer, by understanding and interpreting data science innovation and challenges
Business development, by contributing to need definition and participating in our commercial initiatives

Equipped with the entrepreneurial fibre, you are ready to cooperate in a fast growing company.



In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


University Master Civil Engineer (applied mathematics, IT or similar)
0 to 3 years’ experience as a consultant
Outstanding team player with great interpersonal and communication skills
Strong interest in data science and related sectors such as Energy and Finance
Analytical mind, exploring options and able to synthesize into conclusions
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Dynamic, result oriented, rigorous and committed
Trilingual: French, Dutch and English
Prepared to participate in temporary assignments abroad


Additional Information



We are unable to provide a work permit for this position



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
We're looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to join our client's growing AI team and help design, build, deploy, and maintain production-ready machine learning solutions. In this role, you'll ensure that ML models are not only accurate during experimentation but also reliable, scalable, and maintainable in production across both Microsoft Azure and on-premises environments. Working closely with data engineers, software developers, architects, and business stakeholders, you'll transform machine learning use cases into sustainable technical solutions while supporting compliance with relevant AI regulations.

Key Responsibilities


Prepare, process, and analyse data from multiple internal and external sources to support machine learning initiatives.
Design and implement feature engineering processes while ensuring data quality, consistency, and reproducibility.
Develop, train, validate, and optimise machine learning models for use cases such as classification, regression, forecasting, anomaly detection, and scoring.
Evaluate and benchmark models using appropriate techniques, with a strong focus on performance, explainability, maintainability, and production readiness.
Deploy machine learning models as scalable services, APIs, or batch processes and contribute to standardized deployment practices.
Build and maintain MLOps pipelines, CI/CD processes, and automated deployment workflows for ML solutions.
Monitor model performance, latency, stability, data drift, and model drift, while implementing retraining and feedback mechanisms to ensure long-term effectiveness.
Apply version control to code, models, configurations, and datasets, ensuring reliable and repeatable deployments.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define technical solutions, document implementations, and promote best practices in machine learning engineering, testing, deployment, and monitoring.



Your Profile


Proven experience as a Machine Learning Engineer or in a similar role, delivering production-grade machine learning solutions.
Strong understanding of the complete machine learning lifecycle, from data preparation and model development to deployment and operational support.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure, MLOps practices, CI/CD pipelines, and production deployment of machine learning models.
Experience integrating ML models into backend services, APIs, or data processing workflows.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a pragmatic, results-oriented mindset.
Strong focus on software quality, scalability, maintainability, and operational reliability.
Ability to communicate technical concepts effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
A collaborative approach, strong sense of ownership, and eagerness to continuously learn and adopt new technologies and best practices.
Fluent in either Dutch or French, with a good understanding of the second national language.



What's Offered

This is a hybrid position, requiring two days per week on-site at our client's office and allowing remote work for the remaining three days.

Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INW26885
Pieter Messely
pieter.messely@i4m.be
